item.title: 我如何制作一个在边缘运行的非常快速的链接缩短器 | How I made a really fast Link Shortener that runs on the edge
item.description:
我最近制作了一个名为deoxys的链接缩短器（以非常快的神奇宝贝命名）。 它真的非常快，因为它使用Vercel Edge Functions 。 边缘功能基本上是在云上运行的功能，因此它们非常快并且没有冷启动，并且一切都在服务器上运行，因此客户端负担为零。 在这篇博客中，我将概述 deoxys 的架构。

堆

  • 棱镜
  • 行星秤
  • 打字稿（当然）
  • 尾风CSS
  • 韦尔塞尔

高级概述

前端是用 Next.js 构建的，它是一个全栈React框架。 我使用 tRPC 作为我的 API 层来实现这种甜蜜的类型安全。 如果您不熟悉它，我写了一篇关于 tRPC的博客。 该数据库是由 PlanetScale 提供的 MySQL 数据库（准确地说是 Vitess）。

每当有人缩短新链接时，前端都会调用 tRPC 突变将其存储在数据库中。 我使用的 ORM 是 Prisma，因为它是最好的。

现在有趣的部分来了，每当有人访问缩短的 URL，比如说https://deoxys.nexxel.dev/cat ，它将运行一个边缘函数来检查提供的 slug （在本例中为cat ）是否有效slug，如果是，它会将用户重定向到任何 URL。

代码演练

您可以在此处查看源代码。 它只是一个标准的 Next.js 项目，我还设置了 tRPC 和 Prisma，并连接到我的数据库。

 // prisma/schema.prisma model ShortLink { id Int @id @default(autoincrement()) createdAt DateTime @default(now()) url String @db.VarChar(3000) slug String @unique @@index([slug]) }

这是数据库的架构。 非常简单和最小。 接下来，我创建了一个 API 端点来检查一个 slug 是否有效。 为此，我使用了Next.js API Route 。 我不得不这样做，因为边缘功能不能使用棱镜客户端。 请注意，这是一条动态路由。

 //src/pages/api/get-link/[slug].ts import type { NextApiRequest , NextApiResponse } from " next " ; import { prisma } from " ../../../db/client " ; export default async ( req : NextApiRequest , res : NextApiResponse ) => { const slug = req . query [ " slug " ]; if ( ! slug || typeof slug !== " string " ) { res . status ( 404 ). json ({ message : " please provide a slug " }); return ; } const data = await prisma . shortLink . findFirst ({ where : { slug : { equals : slug , }, }, }); if ( ! data ) { res . status ( 404 ). json ({ message : " short link not found " }); return ; } res . setHeader ( " Content-Type " , " application/json " ); res . setHeader ( " Access-Control-Allow-Origin " , " * " ); res . setHeader ( " Cache-Control " , " s-maxage=1000000000, stale-while-revalidate " ); res . json ( data ); return ; };

如果 slug 有效，它还会将响应缓存 1000000000 秒。 这就是让边缘功能更快的原因。

接下来，我写了我的edge函数，在Next.js中，edge函数写在pages/_middleware.ts

 // src/pages/_middleware.ts import { NextFetchEvent , NextRequest , NextResponse } from " next/server " ; export async function middleware ( req : NextRequest , event : NextFetchEvent ) { if ( req . nextUrl . pathname . startsWith ( " /api/ " ) || req . nextUrl . pathname === " / " ) { return ; } const slug = req . nextUrl . pathname . split ( " / " ). pop (); const fetchSlug = await fetch ( ` ${ req . nextUrl . origin } /api/get-link/ ${ slug } ` ); if ( fetchSlug . status === 404 ) { return NextResponse . redirect ( req . nextUrl . origin ); } const data = await fetchSlug . json (); if ( data ?. url ) { return NextResponse . redirect ( data . url ); } }

它调用该端点并检查 slug 是否有效，如果有效，它将用户重定向到对应于 slug 的 URL。 差不多就是这样。

现在我使用 Tailwind 为它构建了一个不错的 UI。 我还制作了两个 tRPC 端点。 第一个是实时检查以前是否使用过 slug。 我发现这种实时验证非常酷。 看这个。

第二个端点是创建新链接并将其写入数据库。 代码看起来像这样。

 // src/pages/api/trpc/[trpc].ts import * as trpc from " @trpc/server " ; import * as trpcNext from " @trpc/server/adapters/next " ; import { z } from " zod " ; import { prisma } from " ../../../db/client " ; export const appRouter = trpc . router () . query ( " checkSlug " , { input : z . object ({ slug : z . string () }), async resolve ({ input }) { const slugCount = await prisma . shortLink . count ({ where : { slug : { equals : input . slug , }, }, }); return { used : slugCount > 0 }; }, }) . mutation ( " createShortLink " , { input : z . object ({ slug : z . string (), url : z . string () }), async resolve ({ input }) { try { await prisma . shortLink . create ({ data : { slug : input . slug , url : input . url , }, }); } catch ( error ) { console . log ( error ); } }, }); export type AppRouter = typeof appRouter ; export default trpcNext . createNextApiHandler ({ router : appRouter , createContext : () => null , });

我也在这里使用zod进行输入验证。 真的很好的图书馆。

剩下的很简单，我只是制作了一个表单组件来调用我的 tRPC 端点。 首先，我为表单声明了一些状态。

 const [ form , setForm ] = useState < Form > ({ slug : "" , url : "" });

我还在这里调用了我的 tRPC 端点。

 const checkSlug = trpc . useQuery ([ " checkSlug " , { slug : form . slug }], { refetchOnReconnect : false , refetchOnMount : false , refetchOnWindowFocus : false , }); const createShortLink = trpc . useMutation ([ " createShortLink " ]);

表格来了。

 < form onSubmit = { ( event : React . FormEvent < HTMLFormElement > ) => { event . preventDefault (); createShortLink . mutate ({ ... form }); } } className = "mt-6" > { checkSlug . data ?. used ? ( < span className = "font-medium text-center text-red-500" > This link has already been used </ span > ) : ( < span className = "font-medium text-center" > { url } / { form . slug } </ span > ) } { /* ... */ }

在这里，我将一个onSubmit函数传递给调用该 tRPC 突变并在输入中传递表单状态的表单。 这也是我实际实现实时验证used地方，如果端点返回为真，它将使边框变为红色并显示错误消息。

在表单内部只有一堆输入，这是它们的工作方式。

 < input type = "url" value = { form . url } maxLength = { 3000 } onChange = { ( e ) => setForm ({ ... form , url : e . target . value }) } placeholder = "https://duckduckgo.com" className = "block w-full px-4 py-2 font-normal bg-black border-2 border-gray-200 rounded-md focus:outline-none placeholder:text-gray-400" required />

此输入用于必须缩短的 URL，这里我传递了一个onChange函数来设置我的表单状态。 对于随机 slugs，我使用了一个名为 random-word-slugs 的库，它非常酷。 这是随机按钮的代码。

 < input type = " button value = "Random" className = "px-4 py-2 ml-2 font-medium transition-colors duration-300 bg-indigo-500 border-2 border-indigo-500 rounded cursor-pointer hover:bg-transparent" onClick = { () => { const slug = generateSlug (); setForm ({ ... form , slug , }); checkSlug . refetch (); } } />

我也在设置状态，并检查之前是否已经使用过那个特定的 slug。

现在，如果短链接创建成功，则会显示此页面。

这是代码。

 if ( createShortLink . status === " success " ) { return ( < div className = "flex flex-col items-center justify-center mx-3 mt-6" > < span className = "pb-3 text-lg font-semibold" > Here's your link! </ span > < div className = "flex items-center gap-2" > < h1 className = "text-lg text-center md:text-2xl" > { ` ${ url } / ${ form . slug } ` } </ h1 > < button className = "px-4 py-1.5 ml-3 font-medium transition-colors duration-300 bg-indigo-500 border-2 border-indigo-500 rounded hover:bg-transparent" onClick = { () => { copy ( ` ${ url } / ${ form . slug } ` ); } } > Copy </ button > </ div > < button className = "px-4 mt-8 py-1.5 ml-3 font-medium transition-colors duration-300 bg-indigo-500 border-2 border-indigo-500 rounded hover:bg-transparent" onClick = { () => { createShortLink . reset (); setForm ({ slug : "" , url : "" }); } } > Create New </ button > </ div > ); }

所以在这里，如果它返回success ，它会显示缩短的 URL 和复制到剪贴板的按钮。 还有一个创建新按钮，用于重置 tRPC 突变并重置表单状态。

您可以在此处查看此组件的完整代码。

而已。 这有很多变化的部分，我希望我能很好地概述 deoxys 的功能。

网站： https ://deoxys.nexxel.dev
代码： https ://github.com/nexxeln/deoxys

谢谢阅读！



原文:

I recently made a link shortener called deoxys (named after a really fast Pokémon). It's really, really fast because it uses Vercel Edge Functions. Edge functions are basically functions that run on the cloud, so they are really fast and have no cold starts, and everything runs on the server so there is zero client side burden. In this blog I'm going to give you an overview of the architecture of deoxys.

Stack

  • Next.js
  • Prisma
  • PlanetScale
  • tRPC
  • TypeScript (of course)
  • TailwindCSS
  • Vercel

High level overview

The frontend is built with Next.js which is a full stack React framework. I'm using tRPC as my API layer for that sweet type-safety. I wrote a blog about tRPC if you're not familiar with it. The database is a MySQL database (Vitess to be precise) provided by PlanetScale.

Whenever someone shortens a new link, the frontend calls a tRPC mutation to store that in the database. The ORM I'm using is Prisma, because it is simply the best.

Now here comes the interesting part, whenever someone visits a shortened URL, lets say https://deoxys.nexxel.dev/cat, it will run an edge function to check if the provided slug (in this case cat), is a valid slug, if it is, it will redirect the user to whatever the URL was.

Code walkthrough

You can look at the source code here. It's just a standard Next.js project, I also set up tRPC and Prisma, and connected to my database.

// prisma/schema.prisma

model ShortLink {
  id        Int      @id @default(autoincrement())
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  url       String   @db.VarChar(3000)
  slug      String   @unique

  @@index([slug])
}

This is the schema for the database. Very simple and minimal. Next, I made the API endpoint that will check if a slug is valid or not. For this I used a Next.js API Route. I had to do this because the edge function can't use the prisma client. Note that this is a dynamic route.

//src/pages/api/get-link/[slug].ts import type { NextApiRequest, NextApiResponse } from "next"; import { prisma } from "../../../db/client"; export default async (req: NextApiRequest, res: NextApiResponse) => { const slug = req.query["slug"]; if (!slug || typeof slug !== "string") { res.status(404).json({ message: "please provide a slug" }); return; } const data = await prisma.shortLink.findFirst({ where: { slug: { equals: slug, }, }, }); if (!data) { res.status(404).json({ message: "short link not found" }); return; } res.setHeader("Content-Type", "application/json"); res.setHeader("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*"); res.setHeader("Cache-Control", "s-maxage=1000000000, stale-while-revalidate"); res.json(data); return;
};

If the slug is valid, it is also caching the response for 1000000000 seconds. This is what makes the edge function even faster.

Next, I wrote my edge function, in Next.js, edge functions are written in pages/_middleware.ts

// src/pages/_middleware.ts import { NextFetchEvent, NextRequest, NextResponse } from "next/server"; export async function middleware(req: NextRequest, event: NextFetchEvent) { if ( req.nextUrl.pathname.startsWith("/api/") || req.nextUrl.pathname === "/" ) { return; } const slug = req.nextUrl.pathname.split("/").pop(); const fetchSlug = await fetch(`${req.nextUrl.origin}/api/get-link/${slug}`); if (fetchSlug.status === 404) { return NextResponse.redirect(req.nextUrl.origin); } const data = await fetchSlug.json(); if (data?.url) { return NextResponse.redirect(data.url); }
}

It calls that endpoint and checks if the slug is valid, if it is it redirects the user to the URL corresponding to the slug. That's pretty much it.

Now I built a nice UI for it using Tailwind. I also made two tRPC endpoints. The first one is to check if a slug has been previously used before in real-time. I find this real-time validation to be really cool. Look at this.

The second endpoint is to create new links and write it to the database. The code looks like this.

// src/pages/api/trpc/[trpc].ts import * as trpc from "@trpc/server";
import * as trpcNext from "@trpc/server/adapters/next";
import { z } from "zod"; import { prisma } from "../../../db/client"; export const appRouter = trpc .router() .query("checkSlug", { input: z.object({ slug: z.string() }), async resolve({ input }) { const slugCount = await prisma.shortLink.count({ where: { slug: { equals: input.slug, }, }, }); return { used: slugCount > 0 }; }, }) .mutation("createShortLink", { input: z.object({ slug: z.string(), url: z.string() }), async resolve({ input }) { try { await prisma.shortLink.create({ data: { slug: input.slug, url: input.url, }, }); } catch (error) { console.log(error); } }, }); export type AppRouter = typeof appRouter; export default trpcNext.createNextApiHandler({ router: appRouter, createContext: () => null,
});

I'm also using zod for input validation here. Really good library.

The rest was simple, I just made a form component that called my tRPC endpoints. First I declared some state for the form.

const [form, setForm] = useState<Form>({ slug: "", url: "" });

I also called my tRPC endpoints here.

const checkSlug = trpc.useQuery(["checkSlug", { slug: form.slug }], { refetchOnReconnect: false, refetchOnMount: false, refetchOnWindowFocus: false, }); const createShortLink = trpc.useMutation(["createShortLink"]);

Here comes the form.

<form onSubmit={(event: React.FormEvent<HTMLFormElement>) => { event.preventDefault(); createShortLink.mutate({ ...form }); }} className="mt-6" > {checkSlug.data?.used ? ( <span className="font-medium text-center text-red-500"> This link has already been used </span> ) : ( <span className="font-medium text-center"> {url}/{form.slug} </span> )}
{/* ... */}

Here, I'm passing an onSubmit function to the form that calls that tRPC mutation and passes the form state in the input. Also this is where I'm actually implementing that real-time validation, if the endpoint returns used as true, it will make the border red and show the error message.

Inside the form there are just a bunch of inputs, here is how they work.

<input type="url" value={form.url} maxLength={3000} onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, url: e.target.value })} placeholder="https://duckduckgo.com" className="block w-full px-4 py-2 font-normal bg-black border-2 border-gray-200 rounded-md focus:outline-none placeholder:text-gray-400" required
/>

This input is for the URL that has to be shortened, here I'm passing an onChange function to set my form state. Also the type="url" helps in validation.

For random slugs, I'm using a library called random-word-slugs, it's pretty cool. Here's the code for the random button.

<input type="button value="Random" className="px-4 py-2 ml-2 font-medium transition-colors duration-300 bg-indigo-500 border-2 border-indigo-500 rounded cursor-pointer hover:bg-transparent" onClick={() => { const slug = generateSlug(); setForm({ ...form, slug, }); checkSlug.refetch(); }}
/>

The generateSlug() function comes from the random-word-slugs library. I'm also setting the state, and checking if that particular slug has already been used before.

Now if the creation of the short link was successful, it shows this page.

Here's the code for that.

if (createShortLink.status === "success") { return ( <div className="flex flex-col items-center justify-center mx-3 mt-6"> <span className="pb-3 text-lg font-semibold">Here's your link!</span> <div className="flex items-center gap-2"> <h1 className="text-lg text-center md:text-2xl">{`${url}/${form.slug}`}</h1> <button className="px-4 py-1.5 ml-3 font-medium transition-colors duration-300 bg-indigo-500 border-2 border-indigo-500 rounded hover:bg-transparent" onClick={() => { copy(`${url}/${form.slug}`); }} > Copy </button> </div> <button className="px-4 mt-8 py-1.5 ml-3 font-medium transition-colors duration-300 bg-indigo-500 border-2 border-indigo-500 rounded hover:bg-transparent" onClick={() => { createShortLink.reset(); setForm({ slug: "", url: "" }); }} > Create New </button> </div> ); }

tRPC returns the status of a mutation too. So here, if it returns success, it shows the shortened URL and a copy to clipboard button. There is also a create new button that resets the tRPC mutation and resets the form state as well.

You can see the full code for this component here.

That's it. There are a lot of moving parts to this, I hope I gave you nice overview of how deoxys functions.

Website: https://deoxys.nexxel.dev
Code: https://github.com/nexxeln/deoxys

Thanks for reading!
item.author: nexxeln

item.title: Flutter Web 和 Rive 动画教程 | Flutter Web and Rive Animation Tutorial
item.description:
你好！ 这是一个初学者到中级教程，用于设置带有 Rive 动画的 Flutter Web 应用程序。 我最近为 Flutter Hackathon 构建了一个包含 Rive 动画的滑动瓷砖游戏。 在本教程中，我将向您展示如何向 Flutter Web 应用程序添加动画并通过用户输入对其进行控制。

我们将创建一个篮球动画，每次用户单击按钮时都会触发该动画。

首先，我们将创建动画。 如果您已经有了动画，您可以跳到本节，它将向您展示如何将动画添加到 Web 应用程序。

我们将使用 Rive 提供的篮球动画作为他们的教程之一。 这是动画。

打开它时，有一个设计选项卡，可让您修改动画的外观，例如球的颜色和原始形状。

如果切换到“动画”选项卡，您可以看到动画运行。 每个动画都可以有多个“状态”，在这个状态中有一个idle状态，但您可以添加更多，例如rolling状态或您的业务逻辑可能需要的任何状态。

在最新的运行时将动画下载为basketball.riv 。 一旦我们将它连接到 Flutter，我们将能够在用户执行某些操作（例如单击按钮）时播放idle动画。

创建一个我们将添加动画的颤振项目。 创建项目后，如果它尚不存在，则创建一个assets文件夹并将动画文件移动到其中。 我将我的保存在assets/animations/下。

 flutter create fluttertutorial cd fluttertutorial flutter run -d chrome

它将为您启动一个看起来像这样的演示应用程序。

接下来我们添加将帮助我们控制（播放和暂停）动画的库。

添加到pubspec.yaml并运行flutter pub get 。

 dependencies : rive : ^0.8.4

然后在lib/main.dart中，导入包。

 import 'package:rive/rive.dart' ;

现在让我们通过创建一个无状态小部件将动画添加到我们的应用程序中。 将此代码添加到lib/main.dart的末尾

class BasketBallAnimation extends StatelessWidget { const BasketBallAnimation ({ Key ? key }) : super ( key: key ); @override Widget build ( BuildContext context ) { return const Scaffold ( body: Center ( child: RiveAnimation . asset ( 'animations/basketball.riv' )), ); } }

要显示这个类，找到主类MyApp并设置home:指向我们的新小部件。

 class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp ({ Key ? key }) : super ( key: key ); @override Widget build ( BuildContext context ) { return MaterialApp ( title: 'Flutter Demo' , theme: ThemeData ( primarySwatch: Colors . blue , ), home: const BasketBallAnimation (), // make this change ); } }

现在，当您重新运行时，您应该会看到正在播放的篮球动画。

现在让我们给出小部件状态——这就是我们能够判断它是在播放还是暂停的方式。

 class BasketBallAnimation extends StatefulWidget { const BasketBallAnimation ({ Key ? key }) : super ( key: key ); @override State < BasketBallAnimation > createState () => _BasketBallAnimationState (); } class _BasketBallAnimationState extends State < BasketBallAnimation > { bool isPlaying = false ; @override Widget build ( BuildContext context ) { return Scaffold ( body: const Center ( child: RiveAnimation . asset ( 'animations/basketball.riv' )), ); } }

接下来我们将添加一个按钮，当调用该按钮时，将更改isPlaying的值

class _BasketBallAnimationState extends State < BasketBallAnimation > { bool isPlaying = false ; void _toggleAnimation () { setState (() { isPlaying = ! isPlaying ; }); } @override Widget build ( BuildContext context ) { return Scaffold ( body: const Center ( child: RiveAnimation . asset ( 'animations/basketball.riv' )), floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton ( onPressed: _toggleAnimation , tooltip: isPlaying ? 'Pause' : 'Play' , child: Icon ( isPlaying ? Icons . pause : Icons . play_arrow , ), ), } }

最后，我们可以连接 RiveController 方法。 最初，我们将其设置为播放名为idle的动画。 这是一个SimpleAnimation 。 我们将能够通过检查控制器的isActive属性来判断动画是否正在播放。 我们通过将这个控制器传递到我们的动画中，将它附加到我们的动画资产上。

 RiveAnimation . asset ( 'path/to/my/animation.riv' , controllers: [ _controller ])

这是连接控制器的更新代码。

 class _BasketBallAnimationState extends State < BasketBallAnimation > { late RiveAnimationController _controller ; bool get isPlaying => _controller . isActive ; void _toggleAnimation () { setState (() { _controller . isActive = ! _controller . isActive ; }); } @override void initState () { super . initState (); _controller = SimpleAnimation ( 'idle' ); } @override Widget build ( BuildContext context ) { return Scaffold ( body: Center ( child: RiveAnimation . asset ( 'animations/basketball.riv' , controllers: [ _controller ])), floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton ( onPressed: _toggleAnimation , tooltip: isPlaying ? 'Pause' : 'Play' , child: Icon ( isPlaying ? Icons . pause : Icons . play_arrow , ), ), ); } }

现在你应该有一些看起来像下面的屏幕的东西。 当用户与浮动按钮交互时开始和停止的篮球动画。

希望本教程对您有所帮助。 快乐编码:)



原文:

Hi! This is a beginner to intermediate tutorial for setting up a Flutter web app with Rive animations. I recently built a sliding tile game that incorporates Rive animations for the Flutter Hackathon. In this tutorial I’m going to show you how to add an animation to your Flutter web app and control it through user input.

We will create a basketball animation that gets triggered each time the user clicks on a button.

First we’ll create the animation. If you already have your animation you can skip to this section which will show you how to add your animation to a web app.

Rive is a free-to-use platform for designing and creating animations. We are going to use a basketball animation that Rive provides as one of their tutorials. Here is the animation.

When you open it, there’s a Design tab that let’s you modify the look of the animation, like the colours and original shape of the ball.

If you switch to the Animate tab you can see the animation run. Each animation can have multiple ‘states’, in this one there’s an idle state but you could add more, like a rolling state or whatever you might need for your business logic.

Download the animation in the newest runtime as basketball.riv. Once we hook it up to Flutter, we will be able to play the idle animation when the user performs some action, such as clicking a button.

Create a flutter project that we will be adding the animation to. Once the project is created, make an assets folder if it doesn't already exist and move your animation file into it. I saved mine under assets/animations/.

flutter create fluttertutorial
cd fluttertutorial
flutter run -d chrome

It will spin up a demo app for you that looks something like this.

Next we add the library that is going to help us control (play and pause) our animation.

Add to pubspec.yaml and run flutter pub get.

dependencies: rive: ^0.8.4

Then in lib/main.dart, import the package.

import 'package:rive/rive.dart';

Now let’s add the animation to our app by creating a stateless widget. Add this code to the end of lib/main.dart

class BasketBallAnimation extends StatelessWidget { const BasketBallAnimation({Key? key}) : super(key: key); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return const Scaffold( body: Center(child: RiveAnimation.asset('animations/basketball.riv')), ); }
}

To display this class, find the main class, MyApp and set home: to point to our new widget.

class MyApp extends StatelessWidget { const MyApp({Key? key}) : super(key: key); @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return MaterialApp( title: 'Flutter Demo', theme: ThemeData( primarySwatch: Colors.blue, ), home: const BasketBallAnimation(), // make this change ); }
}

Now when you re-run, you should see the basketball animation playing.

Now let’s give the widget state - this is how we will be able to tell if it’s playing or paused.

class BasketBallAnimation extends StatefulWidget { const BasketBallAnimation({Key? key}) : super(key: key); @override State<BasketBallAnimation> createState() => _BasketBallAnimationState();
} class _BasketBallAnimationState extends State<BasketBallAnimation> { bool isPlaying = false; @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( body: const Center(child: RiveAnimation.asset('animations/basketball.riv')), ); }
}

Next we will add a button that, when called, will change the value of isPlaying

class _BasketBallAnimationState extends State<BasketBallAnimation> { bool isPlaying = false; void _toggleAnimation() { setState(() { isPlaying = !isPlaying; }); } @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( body: const Center(child: RiveAnimation.asset('animations/basketball.riv')), floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton( onPressed: _toggleAnimation, tooltip: isPlaying ? 'Pause' : 'Play', child: Icon( isPlaying ? Icons.pause : Icons.play_arrow, ), ), }
}

Finally, we can hook up the RiveController methods. The RiveAnimationController is what holds the state of our animation. Initially, we are going to set it to play our animation named idle. This is a SimpleAnimation. We will be able to tell if the animation is playing or not by checking the isActive property of the controller. We attach this controller to our animation asset by passing it into our animation.

RiveAnimation.asset('path/to/my/animation.riv', controllers: [_controller])

Here is the updated code with the controller hooked up.

class _BasketBallAnimationState extends State<BasketBallAnimation> { late RiveAnimationController _controller; bool get isPlaying => _controller.isActive; void _toggleAnimation() { setState(() { _controller.isActive = !_controller.isActive; }); } @override void initState() { super.initState(); _controller = SimpleAnimation('idle'); } @override Widget build(BuildContext context) { return Scaffold( body: Center( child: RiveAnimation.asset('animations/basketball.riv', controllers: [_controller])), floatingActionButton: FloatingActionButton( onPressed: _toggleAnimation, tooltip: isPlaying ? 'Pause' : 'Play', child: Icon( isPlaying ? Icons.pause : Icons.play_arrow, ), ), ); }
}

Now you should have something that looks like the screen below. A basketball animation that starts and stops when the user interacts with the floating button.

Hope this tutorial helps you. The complete code for lib/main.dart can be found in this gist. Happy coding :)

item.title: 星期一！ | Meme Monday!

item.author: ben

item.title: A11Y 101：悬停和可访问性 | A11Y 101: hover and accessibility
item.description:
在创建令人印象深刻的设计时，我们经常选择实现某些功能以仅在悬停时显示。

这通常会创造出优秀的苦行者，而设计师往往会喜欢这一点。

但是，在可访问性方面，我们应该始终选择替代方案。

这里主要关心的是：
无需悬停就可以访问所有内容吗？

这可能意味着键盘/移动用户没有隐藏元素或后备。

重点呢？

对于某些元素，例如卡片按钮，我们可以利用:focus状态。
虽然这适用于大多数情况，但它有一些只在特定领域工作的缺点。

在卡片的示例中，我们需要将整张卡片包装在一个链接中，虽然这样可行，但我们会失去一些其他机会。

我不会太详细。 有一些使用focus-within的解决方案，但稍后会更多。

对于基础知识，永远不要从元素中删除默认焦点。 许多人倾向于删除它，但是移动和键盘用户如何知道哪个元素处于活动状态？

你需要悬停吗？

除了明显的按钮和链接指示器之外，您可能还有某些仅在用户悬停时显示的显示元素？

就像仅在悬停卡片时出现的更多选项菜单一样。
我建议不要这样做，因为它不是超级包容的。

大多数屏幕阅读器甚至会忽略您的内容（取决于您使用的解决方案）。

那么，你需要隐藏那个按钮吗？
那么，为什么它首先在那里？

如果您发现自己有充分的理由这样做，请确保至少将其展示给屏幕阅读器甚至移动用户！

关于这个主题的一个小注释，甚至还有一种方法可以检测浏览器是否支持鼠标。

注意：这不是 100% 的方法，因为人们可能会选择不使用鼠标，即使它已连接！

 /* The primary input mechanism of the device includes a pointing device of limited accuracy. */ @media ( pointer : coarse ) { ...; } /* The primary input mechanism of the device includes an accurate pointing device. */ @media ( pointer : fine ) { ...; } /* The primary input mechanism of the device does not include a pointing device. */ @media ( pointer : none ) { ...; } /* Primary input mechanism system can hover over elements with ease */ @media ( hover : hover ) { ...; } /* Primary input mechanism cannot hover at all or cannot conveniently hover (eg, many mobile devices emulate hovering when the user performs an inconvenient long tap), or there is no primary pointing input mechanism */ @media ( hover : none ) { ...; } /* One or more available input mechanism(s) can hover over elements with ease */ @media ( any-hover : hover ) { ...; } /* One or more available input mechanism(s) cannot hover (or there are no pointing input mechanisms) */ @media ( any-hover : none ) { ...; }

防止陷阱！

除了所有这些优化的事情，可能发生的最糟糕的事情是你创造了一个陷阱！

你经常在自动模式中看到这种东西。 想想这些时尚网站之一。 他们通常会在悬停时弹出放大的图像预览，对吗？

有时，当您在手机上滚动并点击一次时，会发生这种情况。 该网站没有提供摆脱这种模式的正确方法。

因此，你被困住了！
唯一的出路是刷新网站。

当然，这很糟糕！
我们永远不想让用户停留在我们网站的特定部分。

表单上的工具提示也会发生同样的情况。
是的，这种情况一直在发生。

您正在填写表格，并且该表格的字段上有一些自动建议工具提示。 超级可爱，他们试图帮助我们，但有时他们会出现在球场上。

现在我们失去了输入控件，我们不能再退出信息工具提示，我们甚至不能再专注于该领域了。

当您在选择列表（例如国家/地区列表）上使用某些自动填充机制时，也会发生这种情况。
该网站试图帮助我们自动完成，但它位于该字段的顶部，我们只是失去了对输入的控制。

那么，我该如何改进呢？

主要目标是将您的网站用作键盘和移动用户。

在物理设备上试用。 您是否能够按预期使用所有选项？

您是否能够在不被阻止的情况下填写表格？

如果您可以充分利用您的网站，那么您就在这个层面上取得了成功。
干得好👏

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

When creating impressive designs, we often choose to implement certain features to only show on hover.

This often creates excellent ascetics, and designers tend to love this.

However, we should always opt for alternatives when it comes to accessibility.

The main concern here:
Is everything accessible without hovering?

This could mean no hidden elements or fallback for keyboard/mobile users.

What about focus?

For some elements like, for instance, buttons of cards, we can leverage :focus states.
And although this works for most cases, it has some downsides of only working in specific fields.

In the example of cards, we need to wrap our whole card in a link, and while that works, we then lose some other opportunities.

I won't go too much into detail. There are some solutions to using focus-within, but more on that at a later stage.

For the basics, never remove the default focus from your elements. Many people tend to remove it, but how will mobile and keyboard users know which element is active?

Do you need hover?

Besides the apparent button and link indicators, you might have certain display elements that only show when users hover?

Like a more options menu that only appears on hovering a card.
I would advise against this as it's not super inclusive.

Most screen readers will even ignore your content (depending on what solution you used).

So, do you need to hide that button?
Then, why was it there in the first place?

If you find yourself having a good reason for that, make sure you atleast show it to screen readers and even mobile users!

A small note on this topic, there is even a way to detect if a browser has mouse support.

Note: This is not a 100% way of doing it, as people might choose not to use a mouse even when it's attached!

/* The primary input mechanism of the device includes a pointing device of limited accuracy. */
@media (pointer: coarse) { ...;
} /* The primary input mechanism of the device includes an accurate pointing device. */
@media (pointer: fine) { ...;
} /* The primary input mechanism of the device does not include a pointing device. */
@media (pointer: none) { ...;
} /* Primary input mechanism system can hover over elements with ease */
@media (hover: hover) { ...;
} /* Primary input mechanism cannot hover at all or cannot conveniently hover (e.g., many mobile devices emulate hovering when the user performs an inconvenient long tap), or there is no primary pointing input mechanism */
@media (hover: none) { ...;
} /* One or more available input mechanism(s) can hover over elements with ease */
@media (any-hover: hover) { ...;
} /* One or more available input mechanism(s) cannot hover (or there are no pointing input mechanisms) */
@media (any-hover: none) { ...;
}

(Big shoutout to Blackbam for this complete answer)

Prevent traps!

Besides all those optimization things, the worst thing that could happen is that you create a trap!

You often see this kind of thing with auto-modals. Think about one of these fashion websites. They usually popup a zoomed-in image preview on hover, right?

Sometimes this happens when you scroll on your mobile and tap once. The website doesn't provide the right ways of getting out of this modal.

Hence, you are trapped!
The only way out is to refresh the website.

This is, of course, bad!
We never want to get a user stuck on a specific part of our website.

The same can happen with tooltips on forms.
And yes, this happens all the time.

You are filling out a form, and the form has some autosuggestions tooltips on the field. Super lovely they are trying to help us, but sometimes they pop up on the field.

And now we lost input controls, we can no longer exit the info tooltip, and we can't even focus on the field anymore.

This also happens when you use some autofill mechanisms on select lists, for instance, the country ones.
The website is trying to help us with a autocomplete, but it sits on top of the field, and we just lost control of the input.

So, how can I improve?

The main objective is to use your website as a keyboard and mobile user.

Try it out on a physical device. Are you able to use all the options as intended?

Are you able to fill out a form without getting blocked?

If you can use your website to the fullest, you are succeeding on this level.
Well done 👏

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
item.author: dailydevtips1

item.title: Magento 性能优化：使 Magento 存储更快的技巧 | Magento Performance Optimization: Tips to Make Magento Store Faster
item.description:
无论您拥有大型或小型电子商店，商店的表现都会对转化率产生不利影响。 亚马逊和谷歌所做的详细研究甚至证实了这一点。 所以，如果你想提高转化率，Magento 性能优化肯定能帮到你。

但是，如果您的商店业绩不佳，您将无法从该商店获得最大收益。

以下是 Magento 2 速度优化的一些提示和技巧，以加快 Magento 2 商店的速度。 因此，请抽出一些时间继续阅读以了解更多信息！

有许多因素决定您的电子商务商店的成功。 而且，加载速度就是其中之一。 这是因为如果您的客户发现速度较慢，他们总是会犹豫从您的商店购买任何东西。 因此，优化您的商店确实有助于重新获得客户的信任以及增强用户体验。
以下是一些提高 Magento 商店性能和转化率的技巧。

提示 1. 加快初始连接建立过程

加快浏览器和服务器之间的连接建立时间也可以帮助您。 实现相同目的的最佳方法是负载平衡方法。 它不仅有助于减少花费在建立连接上的时间，而且还可以加快从浏览器下载内容的过程。

技巧 2. 减少第一个字节的时间

整页缓存是改进此索引的最佳方式。 它减少了 TTFB，这有助于浏览器更快地下载页面内容及其资源。

提示 3. 减少商店使用的图像大小

您知道超过 60% 的网页大小包含图像吗？ 因此，如果您优化网站上使用的图像（产品），它可以减少网页的整体大小。 因此，加快 Magento 2 商店的速度。

技巧 4. 减少请求的数量

减少请求数量还可以帮助您提高商店绩效。 以下是您可以采取的一些步骤来实现相同的目标：

  • 将 JS 和 CSS 文件合并为单个文件
  • 仅将小资源放入页面正文
  • 删除所有不必要的资源
  • 将图像合并为精灵

有许多解决方案提供商提供 Magento 速度优化服务，CedCommerce 就是其中之一。 他们提供了许多高性能的Magento 扩展来帮助您的商店表现得更好。 因此，如果您需要帮助，请随时咨询，无需支付任何费用！

提示 5. 加快页面资源的下载速度

页面资源的下载速度会对商店的性能产生重大影响。 好消息是您可以通过多种方式做到这一点，主要的方法如下所述：

  • 启用 HTTP 压缩
  • 优化商店中的图像
  • 减小 JS、CSS 和 HTML 内容的大小
  • 申请 CDN
  • 将 Javascript 移动到页面正文的末尾

技巧 6. 提高 CPU 和内存使用率

您可以通过跟踪过去 30 天的 CPU 和内存使用情况来分析您的 Magento 站点性能。 通过选择正确的服务，您可以跟踪和定义影响商店绩效的所有瓶颈。 它最终将有助于纠正它们。

提示 7. 使用兼容的主题和第三方扩展

如果您使用主题或第三方扩展，那么它们与您的商店兼容很重要。 否则会减慢页面加载速度，影响整体性能。

技巧 8. 启用清漆缓存

它使用其功能来减少来自商店的未来等效请求的整体响应和 CPU 加载时间。

加起来

这里提到的技巧对 Magento 性能优化都有帮助。 遵循这些步骤将帮助您加速 Magento 2 商店并改善用户体验并提高客户满意度。 我们还想补充一点，如果您仍然发现您的商店存在问题，您必须咨询并采取商店优化等服务。 它将节省您大量的时间和精力，并使整个过程对您来说轻而易举。



原文:

Whether you have a big or small e-shop, the performance of a store can have an adverse impact on conversion rates. This is even confirmed by the detailed research done by Amazon and Google. So, if you want to increase your conversion rate, Magento performance optimization can surely help you.

Magento is a common and widely popular platform used by a majority of eCommerce website owners because of the benefits and flexibility it offers over others. But if your store performance is low, you won’t get the maximum benefit out of this store.

Here are some tips and tricks for Magento 2 speed optimization to speed up Magento 2 store. So, spare some time and read on to learn more!

Top Tips for Magento Performance Optimization

There are many factors that decide the success of your eCommerce store. And, loading speed is one of them. It is because your customers will always hesitate to buy anything from your store if they find it slower. So, getting your store optimized can really help in regaining your customer’s trust along with enhancing user experience.
Here are some tips to increase your Magento store performance and conversion rate.

Tip 1. Speed Up Initial Connection Building Process

Speeding up the connection building time between the browser and server can also help you. The best possible way to achieve the same is by the method of Load Balancing. Not only will it help improve time spent on building connections but also speed up the process of downloading content from the browser.

Tip 2. Reduce Time To First Byte

TTFB (Time to First Byte) is another key indicator of web page speed that reflects the time period between the initial request to a server and its response. Full Page Caching is the best way to improve this index. It reduces the TTFB which helps browsers download page content and its resources faster.

Tip 3. Reduce Images Size used on Store

Do you know over 60% of the web page size comprises images? So, if you optimize images (product) used on your website, it can reduce the overall size of the web page. And hence, speed up the speed of your Magento 2 store.

Tip 4. Reduce The Number Of Requests

Reducing the number of requests can also help you improve your store performance. Here are some steps you can take to achieve the same:

  • Merge JS and CSS files into single files
  • Place only the small resources into page body
  • Remove all unnecessary resources
  • Merge images into sprites

There are many solution providers who offer Magento speed optimization services, CedCommerce being one of them. They offer many high-performing Magento extensions to help your store perform better. So, if you need help, feel free to get a consultation without paying any charges!

Tip 5. Speed Up Downloading Of Page Resources

The downloading speed of page resources can have a major effect on your stores’ performance. The good news is you can do it in multiple ways, the major ones are mentioned below:

  • Enable the HTTP compression
  • Optimize images on your store
  • Reduce the sizes of JS, CSS, and HTML content
  • Apply CDN
  • Mover Javascript to the end of page body

Tip 6. Improve CPU And Memory Usage

You can analyze your Magento site performance by tracking the CPU and Memory usage for the last 30 days. By opting for the right services, you can track and define all the bottlenecks that are affecting your store performance. It will ultimately help rectify them.

Tip 7. Use Compatible Theme and Third-Party Extensions

If you are using themes or third-party extensions, it is important that they are compatible with your store. Otherwise, it can slow down the page loading speed, affecting the overall performance.

Tip 8. Enable Varnish Cache

Varnish Cache is an HTTP accelerator app that performs reverse proxy-server caching. It uses its capabilities to reduce the overall response and CPU loading time for future equivalent requests from your store.

Summing Up

The tips mentioned here are all helpful in Magento performance optimization. Following these steps will help you speed up Magento 2 store and improve the user experience and enhance customer satisfaction. We will also like to add that if you still find problems in your store, you must consult and take services like Store optimization. It will save a lot of your time and effort as well as make the whole process a piece of cake for you.
item.author: jyoti_

item.title: CTO 最后一天：反思、错误和一些学习 | CTO last day: reflections, mistakes, and some learnings
item.description:
在担任该职位大约两年后，我于 2021 年 4 月辞去了在 Akvo 担任首席技术官的工作。

今天， 六七八九十个月后，似乎是时候停止拖延并写点什么了。

离开

当周日晚上成为一周中最糟糕的部分时，我意识到我需要离开，那时我的感觉与我以前的学生时代完全一样：焦虑和不开心。

但这不仅仅是周日晚上。 我从不介意在工作时间之外突然出现一些工作问题——我喜欢解决问题——但现在它只会吸走我所做的所有事情的所有乐趣。

当然，这一切带来的睡眠问题。

真正的幸福只有在您发现自己喜欢的问题并乐于解决时才会出现。
马克·曼森（Mark Manson），不给 F*ck 的微妙艺术。

根本原因分析

无需通过5 个为什么来查看原因：公司财务状况的复合效应、我参与的决策的影响以及完全没有准备的感觉。

例如，我对新业务战略的翻译：停止产品开发。

自十二年前公司成立以来一直是该公司的旗舰产品，我该推动谁停止开发？ 更有经验的 CTO 会不会有不同的看法？ 更有经验的 CTO 会改变业务战略吗？ 改变了产品开发的融资方式？ 合同是如何谈判的？ 寻找外部投资？

以及对开发团队的影响……

这是我帮助做出的最后一个决定。

错误和教训

强制性“经验教训”部分：

  1. 再见编码。
  2. 投诉很好。
  3. 清晰是一项艰苦的工作。
  4. 业务第一，技术第二。
  5. 冲突：避免成为坏掉的电话。
  6. 艰难的对话（有数据）。
  7. 始终是系统问题。
  8. 自责是没有意义的。
  9. 这都是关于团队的。
  10. 不要进行更改，进行实验。
  11. 反馈循环很长。
  12. 围栏是必要的邪恶。
  13. 孤独。
  14. 我不讨厌人事管理！

再见编码

尽管很痛苦，但总会有比编码更重要的事情。

投诉很好

不是所有的抱怨，也不是一直抱怨，但一定程度的抱怨意味着人们足够信任你，他们对工作足够关心。

投诉是一个机会：

  1. 做出改进。
  2. 提供清晰度。

最好的结果是在给予一些额外的明确说明后委托投诉者的改进。

清晰是一项艰苦的工作

不仅沟通是混乱的，而且你需要重复自己十次，首先要自己找到清晰的内容需要大量的工作。

业务第一，技术第二

我明白我作为 CTO 的主要职责是改进我们交付软件的方式。 重大错误。

我应该从一开始就深入研究业务如何运作以及软件如何支持和启用它。

冲突：避免成为坏掉的电话

您与 Bar 谈论 Buzz，但 Bar 说 Quux，您对此一无所知。 因此，您回到 Foo 以澄清 Bar 的 Buzz 的 Quux 和 Foo 回复 Flob。

多么浪费时间、精力和混乱的根源。

根本问题是 Foo 不想与 Bar 进行艰难的对话，而我对此深表同情，这需要发生。

因此，根据情况：

  1. 让 Foo 与 Bar 进行一次艰难的对话。
  2. 与 Foo 和 Bar 坐在一起进行艰难的对话。

困难的对话（有数据）

有两个因素使艰难的对话变得不那么困难：

总是系统问题

在目前的情况下，每个人都在尽力而为。
考虑到这一点，一旦你从责备他人转变为理解、检查和改变系统，对话就会变得更加富有成效。

步骤：

  1. 说明我们正试图了解系统如何导致结果。
  2. 重复第 1 点。
  3. 同意结果是不可取的。
  4. 收集有关系统行为的时间线/事实，以了解“情况”。
  5. 就理想的结果达成一致。
  6. 同意需要哪些系统更改来避免（1）和促进（2）。

请注意，“情况”可能是工作人员缺乏知识或能力。 这是可以修复的。

改变系统，人们就会跟随。

自责是没有意义的

我一直认为承认“这是我的错！” 这样做是一件勇敢的事，但如果你真的相信某件事是你的错，那么你就准备好相信其他人也有过错。

责备和自责会适得其反。 始终是系统问题。

一切都与团队有关

什么团队、组成、职责、内部和外部动态……这将是您进行系统更改的主要杠杆点。

不要进行更改，进行实验

变化是可怕的，并伴随着很多阻力，但进行实验会发出完全不同的信号：这是一个旨在学习的暂时性事物。

实验预计会“失败”，这带来了诚实反馈所需的心理安全，并增加了参与的意愿。

反馈是高质量的，因为它来自经验证据，而不是理论上的“这在这里永远行不通”。

而最重要的原因：

改变文化首先要改变人们的行为，而不是人们的想法。
反馈循环很长

你会错过那些 72 小时的测试套件。

围栏是必要的邪恶

我犯了好几次错误，要求人们做重要但不紧急的工作，并期望他们能抽出时间去做。 它很少奏效。

尽管让我很痛苦，但解决方法是明确限制人们的时间，这样如果出现其他冲突的优先级，他们可以依靠你的权威来推回，或者将冲突升级给你。

孤独

我从来没有这么多话，开过这么多会议，但你们公司里没有人会和你们一样，所以我有时真的很孤独。

我最终在 LinkedIn 上联系了随机的 CTO，这非常有用。

我不讨厌人事管理！

你可能不在乎，但这对我来说是一个很大的惊喜。

即使 1-2-1 天真的很累，我真的很享受与其他人力资源共度的时光。

接下来是什么？

这种体验就像我的第一杯啤酒、第一篇博文或第一次会议演讲。

尝试一次还不足以让我下定决心。

但在开始新工作之前，最初的计划是在 2021 年剩下的时间里休息，从工作和 COVID 中得到喘息，并在我妻子经历一些令人讨厌的手术时照顾我的家人。

但生活还有其他计划......



原文:

In April 2021, I left my job at Akvo as CTO, after about two years in the position.

Today, six seven eight nine ten months later, it seems like about time to stop procrastinating and write something about it.

Leaving

I realised that I needed to leave when Sunday evenings became the worst part of the week when I felt exactly the same as in my old school days: anxious and unhappy.

But it was not only Sunday evenings. I have never minded some work problem popping in my head out of working hours -- I love solving problems -- but now it will just suck all the joy of whatever I was doing.

And of course, the sleeping troubles that came with all of this.

True happiness occurs only when you find the problems you enjoy having and enjoy solving.
Mark Manson, The Subtle Art of Not Giving a F*ck.

Root Cause Analysis

No need to go through the 5 whys to see what the cause was: the compounded effect of the company’s financial situation, the impact of the decisions I was involved with, and the feeling of being completely unprepared.

As an example, my translation of the new business strategy: stop product development.

Who I was to push for halting the development of what has been the company’s flagship product since its inception, twelve years ago? Would a more experienced CTO see otherwise? Would a more experienced CTO change the business strategy? Changed how product development was financed? How contracts were negotiated? Find external investment?

And the impact on the development team …

This was the last decision that I helped make.

Mistakes and lessons learned

Mandatory “lessons learned” section:

  1. Bye-bye coding.
  2. Complaints are good.
  3. Clarity is hard work.
  4. Business first, tech second.
  5. Conflicts: avoid being a broken telephone.
  6. Difficult conversations (with data).
  7. It is always a system problem.
  8. Self-blaming is pointless.
  9. It is all about teams.
  10. Don't make changes, run experiments.
  11. Feedback loops are loooong.
  12. Ring-fencing is a necessary evil.
  13. Lonely.
  14. I don’t dislike people management!

Bye-bye coding

As much as it hurts, there will be always something more important than coding.

Complaints are good

Not all complains and not all the time, but some level of complaining means that people trust you enough and that they care enough about the job.

Complains are an opportunity to either:

  1. Make an improvement.
  2. Provide clarity.

Best outcome is delegating the improvement in the complainer after giving some additional clarity.

Clarity is hard work

Not only communication is messy and you will need to repeat yourself ten times, finding the clarity yourself in the first place requires a lot of work.

Business first, tech second

I understood that my main responsibility as CTO was to improve how we delivered software. Major mistake.

I should have dug deeper from the very beginning on how the business works and how software supported and enabled it.

Conflicts: avoid being a broken telephone

Foo complains about Bar because of Buzz. You talk with Bar about Buzz but Bar says Quux, which you have no clue about. So you go back to Foo to clarify Bar's Buzz's Quux and Foo replies Flob.

What a waste of time, energy, and a source of confusion.

The underlying issue is that Foo does not want to have a difficult conversation with Bar, which, as much I can empathise with, it needs to happen.

So either, depending on circumstances:

  1. Ask Foo to have a difficult conversation with Bar.
  2. Sit with Foo and Bar to have a difficult conversation.

Difficult conversations (with data)

Two ingredients that made difficult conversations less difficult to have:

It is always a system problem

Everybody is doing their best, under the current circumstances.
Gerald M. Weinberg, Becoming a Technical Leader

With this in mind, it is amazing how much more productive conversations are once you move from blaming people to understanding, inspecting, and changing the system.

The steps:

  1. State that we are trying to understand how the system leads to the outcome.
  2. Repeat point 1.
  3. Agree that the outcome was undesirable.
  4. Collect the timeline/facts about the system behavior, to understand "the circumstances".
  5. Agree on what was the desirable outcome.
  6. Agree on what system change is needed to avoid (1) and promote (2).

Note that "circumstances" can be a lack of knowledge or competency by the person doing the work. This is something that can be fixed.

Change the system and people will follow.

Self-blaming is pointless

I always thought that acknowledging that "It is my fault!" was a brave thing to do, but if you really believe that something is your fault, then you are ready to believe that others can be at fault.

Blaming and self-blaming are counterproductive. It is always a system problem.

It is all about teams

What teams, composition, responsibilities, the internal and external dynamics, ... this is going to be your main leverage point for system change.

Don't make changes, run experiments

Change is scary and comes with a lot of resistance, but running an experiment sends a different completely different signal: it is a temporal thing aimed to learn.

Experiments are expected to "fail", which brings the psychological safety required for honest feedback and increases the willingness to participate.

Feedback is high quality as it comes from empirical evidence, not theoretical "this will never work here".

And the most important reason:

Change culture by first changing what people do, not how people think.
John Shook, How to Change a Culture: Lessons from NUMMI

Feedback loops are loooong

You will miss those 72 hours test suites.

Ring-fencing is a necessary evil

I made several times the mistake of asking people for important-but-not-urgent work and expecting them to find the time for it. It rarely worked.

As much as it pains me, the workaround is to explicitly ring-fence people's time, so that if other conflicting priority arises, they can rely on your authority to push back, or escalate the conflict to you.

Lonely

I have never talked so much and been in so many meetings, but nobody in your company will be in your same position, so I actually felt quite lonely at times.

I ended up reaching out to random CTOs on LinkedIn, which was surprisingly useful.

I don’t dislike people management!

You probably do not care but it was a big surprise for me.

Even if the 1-2-1 days were really really draining, I actually enjoyed the time spent with other human resources.

What is next?

The experience was like my first beer, first blog post or first conference talk.

Trying once is not enough to make up my mind.

But before starting a new job, the original plan was to take the rest of 2021 off, get a respite from work and COVID, and take care of my family while my wife went through some nasty surgery.

But life had other plans ...

item.title: javascript中的解构赋值🥶 | Destructuring assignment in javascript 🥶
item.description:
严厉的帕利瓦尔

发表于来自 ES6 的超级酷和超级有用的功能，尽管这是一个很大的话题😂我会尽量让它简短易懂

让我们开始吧

什么是解构。

解构用于将复杂的结构分解成碎片。 就像拆包一样。

让我们用我们的第一个例子来破坏

const obj = { name : ' bla bla ' , car : ' supra ' } // Destructuring assignment const { name } = obj // bla bla

虽然我们也可以访问这样的值console.log(obj.name)那么到底有什么意义呢？ 关键是现在我们创建了一个单独的变量而不是数组成员。 现在我们可以随时随地直接访问 name 🔥 我们可以通过解构做一些更强大的事情。 

 let [ name , lastname ] = ' harshit paliwal ' . split ( ' ' ) console . log ( name ) //harshit console . log ( lastname ) //paliwal

解构赋值通过将项目复制到变量中来工作。 它不会修改数组，它只是一种更短的写入方式。 如同

const arr = ' harshit paliwal ' . split ( ' ' ) console . log ( arr [ 0 ]) //harshit console . log ( arr [ 1 ]) //paliwal

如果我们更改变量的值，我们创建的变量只是 obj/array 的副本，它不会影响原始对象或数组。 我们可以随时重新分配价值。 除了嵌套解构之外，我们还可以做很多事情

const obj = { superHero : { name : " iron man " , power : { powerOne : ' rich ' , powerTwo : ' super genius ' } } } const { superHero :{ name , power :{ powerOne , powerTwo }}} = obj console . log ( name ) //iron man console . log ( powerOne ) //rich console . log ( powerTwo ) //super genius

结论

解构是一项强大的功能，可用于使您的代码更具可读性和简洁性。
如果您没有在代码中使用它，我鼓励您尝试一下。 快乐编码

资源

伊曼纽尔·博阿滕—— May 31 5月31日



原文:

Harshit paliwal

Posted on Jun 1

Super cool and Super helpful feature from ES6, even though it is a big topic 😂 I will try to make it short and easy

lets get started

What is destructuring.

destructuring is used to break down complex structures into pieces. It's like unpacking a bag.

lets destucture with our first example

const obj ={ name:'bla bla', car:'supra'
} // Destructuring assignment
const { name } = obj // bla bla

though we can access the value like this alsoconsole.log(obj.name) so what exactly is the point? the point is now we created a separate variable instead of array members. now we can directly access name wherever and whenever we want 🔥 some more powerful things we can do with destructuring.

let [name,lastname] = 'harshit paliwal'.split(' ') console.log(name) //harshit
console.log(lastname) //paliwal

destructuring assignment works by copying an item into a variable. It doesn't modify the array its just a shorter way to write. same as

const arr = 'harshit paliwal'.split(' ') console.log(arr[0]) //harshit
console.log(arr[1]) //paliwal

the variable we created is just a copy of obj/array if we change the value of the variable it doesn't affect the original object or array. we can reassign value whenever we want. 🔥

const obj = {
name:'harshit'
}
let {name} = obj;
name = 'jone'
console.log(name) // jone

There is a lot more than that we can do nested destructuring also

const obj = { superHero: { name: "iron man", power: { powerOne: 'rich', powerTwo: 'super genius' } }
} const {superHero:{name, power:{powerOne, powerTwo}}} = obj console.log(name) //iron man
console.log(powerOne) //rich
console.log(powerTwo) //super genius

Conclusion

Destructuring is a powerful feature that can be used to make your code more readable and concise.
if you are not using it in your code I encourage you to give it a try. Happy coding

Resource

javascript MDN

javascript.info

Emmanuel Boateng - May 31

item.title: Flutter - 无线设备 | Flutter - Wireless device
item.description:
牛小可可兰

发表于要求：

  • 活跃的 sus herramientas de desarrollador。
  • 洛格拉姆斯🚀🚀🚀

    雅库布·安德杰耶夫斯基—— May 23 5月23日



原文:

Ushieru Kokoran

Posted on Jun 1

Requisitos:

  • Android 11 (o superior)
  • Flutter (Instalado y configurado)
  • Active sus herramientas de desarrollador.
  • Dentro de sus herramienta de desarrollador active depuración inalámbrica (Wireless debugging) esta un par de opciones debajo de depuración USB (USB debugging).
  • Asegúrese que esta su PC y su móvil en la misma red.

  • Entre a depuración inalámbrica, ahi le mostrara la ip y el puerto des dispositivo.

  • Asegúrese de tener el comando adb en su terminal.

Si no lo tienes, no te preocupes, agrégalo a tus variables de entorno. Si tienes Android Studio instalado la ruta debería ser algo como -> C:\Users*TU_USUARIO*\AppData\Local\Android\Sdk\platform-tools\

$ adb connect [IP]:[PORT]

  • Ahora la prueba de fuego, corre algún proyecto que tengas por ahí. Para fines prácticos yo usare un Hello world de flutter.

Lo logramos 🚀🚀🚀

Ya solo queda cerrar la conexión de adb (No obligatorio si recomendado).

Que te pareció? Necesitas ayuda con algo? déjalo en los comentarios.

Mucha suerte en tus proyectos y Happy Hacking 🧑‍💻🎉

Jakub Andrzejewski - May 23

item.title: 让我们通过实际示例学习 Git 重置 | Let’s Learn Git Reset With Practical Example
item.description:
通过我们与 git 作为开发人员的工作流程，我们偶尔需要撤消对代码所做的更改，这就是我们想要使用 git reset 的时候。

但是，老实说，有时使用 git reset 会让人感到困惑，甚至令人恐惧。 我曾经发现这个命令是一个“黑匣子”，我不知道它到底做了什么，但我知道我很害怕我可能会删除我想要保留的代码部分，甚至以某种方式删除破坏整个项目。

在这篇文章中，我们将大致了解 git，特别是 git reset，以了解它。 因为，就像我们生活中的很多事情一样，我们害怕我们不理解的东西。

注意：写这篇文章的前提是你至少对 git 有基本的了解并知道它的基本命令。

我们可以将 git 视为管理三棵树的内容管理器：
在我们使用“git add”命令后，我们执行该命令的文件将进入暂存区。
注意： Head 和 Index 将它们的文件存储在 .git 目录中。

基本生命周期

通常，在将新文件添加到我们的存储库后，我们将有以下循环：

  1. 对我们的文件进行更改。
  2. 暂存我们的文件。
  3. 提交我们的文件。
  4. 将我们的文件推送到我们的远程存储库。

步骤 1发生在工作树中。 我们进行了更改，但尚未进行这些更改。

第 2 步发生在索引树中。 我们上演了我们所做的更改，但我们还没有提交这些更改。

第 3 步发生在头树中。 从那里，我们将这些更改推送到我们的远程存储库。

使用 git 重置

现在我们对 git 有了更好的理解，我们可以继续执行 git reset 命令。 为此，我们为 git reset 命令获得了三个“标志”：

  1. 软：从头树中删除更改，并将它们保留在索引和工作目录树中。
  2. 混合：从头树和索引树中删除更改，并将它们保留在工作目录树中。
  3. 困难：从所有三棵树中删除更改。

所以让我们看一个例子。 我们有一个初始化的 git repo，我们在 repo 中创建了一个名为 file1.txt 的文件。 当我们运行 git status 时，我们得到：

 git status On branch master No commits yet Untracked files: (use “git add <file>…” to include in what will be committed) file1.txt nothing added to commit but untracked files present (use “git add” to track)

现在我们的文件只存在于工作目录中，而不存在于索引中。 它是“未跟踪的”。 让我们将它添加到我们的索引中，然后重新运行 git status：

 git commit -m “our first file” [master (root-commit) 7ed5dc0] our first file 1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-) create mode 100644 file1.txt git status On branch master nothing to commit, working tree clean

正如我们在最后一个 git status 之后看到的，没有什么可以提交的。 在这个阶段，所有三棵树都是同步的。

现在，让我们在名为 file2.txt 的目录中添加另一个文件，我们将再次完成整个循环：

 git status On branch master Untracked files: (use “git add <file>…” to include in what will be committed) file2.txt nothing added to commit but untracked files present (use “git add” to track) git add file2.txt git status On branch master Changes to be committed: (use "git restore --staged <file>..." to unstage) new file: file2.txt git commit -m "added file 2" [master 742c271] added file 2 1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-) create mode 100644 file2.txt git status On branch master nothing to commit, working tree clean

我们可以使用 git log 查看我们的两个提交：

 git log commit 742c2713887c8b0056bea68d32f542807dca2324 (HEAD -> master) Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com> Date: Wed May 25 22:39:34 2022 +0300 added file 2 commit 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225 Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com> Date: Wed May 25 22:34:23 2022 +0300 our first file

现在让我们使用 git reset 使用提交 ID 回到我们的第一个提交。 首先，我们将在没有任何标志的情况下尝试它：

 git reset 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225 // this is the first commit id

现在让我们重新运行 git status：

 git status On branch master Untracked files: (use “git add <file>…” to include in what will be committed) file2.txt

当我们在没有任何标志的情况下运行 git reset 时，git 默认使用混合标志。 这意味着我们第二次提交的更改已从 Head 和 Index 中删除，但仍保留在工作目录中。 让我们再次暂存更改并提交它们：

 git add file2.txt git commit -m “added file 2 again” [master c211158] added file 2 again 1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-) create mode 100644 file2.txt

好的。 现在当我们运行 git log 时，我们只会看到两个提交，第一个提交和第二个提交：

 git log commit c211158cee7a04c1cba4c7e10a2474c64b298d1c (HEAD -> master) Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com> Date: Wed May 25 23:42:56 2022 +0300 added file 2 again // this is the new commit commit 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225 Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com> Date: Wed May 25 22:34:23 2022 +0300 our first file

现在我们将使用 git reset，但这次使用硬标志：

 git reset — hard 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225 HEAD is now at 7ed5dc0 our first file git status On branch master nothing to commit, working tree clean

当使用“硬”标志时，我们删除了提交后返回的所有内容。 因为我们在那次提交之后创建了另一个文件，所以该文件消失了：

没有 file2.txt 了。

结论

当我们了解它的作用时，使用 git reset 会很有帮助。 我强烈建议阅读更多关于它的内容并尝试在安全的环境中使用它，就像我在本教程中所做的那样。 我真的相信这是最好的学习方式，举几个例子。

本文最初发表于Medium



原文:

Through our workflow with git as developers, we occasionally need to undo changes that we did to our code, and that is when we want to use git reset.

But, if I’m being honest, sometimes that can be confusing, and even scary, to use git reset. I used to find this command as a “black box” that I don’t know exactly what it does, but I do know that I’m scared that I might delete somehow parts of my code that I want to keep, or even somehow destroy the entire project.

In this post, we will take a look at git in general, and specifically git reset, to understand it. Because, like a lot of things in our life, we are afraid of what we don’t understand.

Note: This post was written assuming you have at least a basic familiarity with git and know basic commands of it.

How git works

We can think about git as a content manager that manages three trees:
1. The Head: points to our last commit.
2. The index: points to our next commit. After we use the “git add” command, the files we performed that command on are going to the staging area.
3. The working Directory(Working Tree): The place we work on our actual files in the project, before adding them to our stating area.

Note: The Head and the Index store their files inside the .git directory.

Basic Lifecycle

Usually, after adding a new file to our repository, we will have the following cycle:

  1. Make changes to our file.
  2. Stage our file.
  3. Commit our file.
  4. Push our file to our remote repository.

Step 1 is happening in the working tree. We made changes but did not stage those yet.

Step 2 is happening in the index tree. We staged the changes that we did, but we did not commit those yet.

step 3 is happening in the head tree. From there, we push those changes to our remote repository.

Using git reset

Now that we understand git better, we can continue to the git reset command. There are two parts to git reset:

1. The commit we go back to.
2. In which trees we want to keep our changes.

To get there, we get three “flags” for the git reset command:

  1. Soft: remove the changes from the head tree, and keep them in the index and the working directory trees.
  2. Mixed: remove the changes from the head and the index trees, and keep them in the working directory tree.
  3. Hard: remove the changes from all three trees.

So let’s look at an example. We have an initialized git repo, we created one file in the repo named file1.txt. When we run git status we get:

git status
On branch master
No commits yet
Untracked files:
(use “git add <file>…” to include in what will be committed)
file1.txt
nothing added to commit but untracked files present (use “git add” to track)

Right now our file exists only in the working directory, but not in the index. It is “untracked”. Let’s add it to our index, and rerun git status:

git commit -m “our first file”
[master (root-commit) 7ed5dc0] our first file
1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-)
create mode 100644 file1.txt
git status
On branch master
nothing to commit, working tree clean

As we can see after the last git status, there is nothing to commit. At this stage, all three trees are synced.

Now, let’s add another file to our directory named file2.txt, and we will go through the entire cycle again:

git status
On branch master
Untracked files:
(use “git add <file>…” to include in what will be committed)
file2.txt
nothing added to commit but untracked files present (use “git add” to track)

git add file2.txt

git status
On branch master
Changes to be committed:
(use "git restore --staged <file>..." to unstage)
new file:   file2.txt

git commit -m "added file 2"
[master 742c271] added file 2
1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-)
create mode 100644 file2.txt

git status
On branch master
nothing to commit, working tree clean

We can see our two commits with git log:

git log
commit 742c2713887c8b0056bea68d32f542807dca2324 (HEAD -> master)
Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com>
Date: Wed May 25 22:39:34 2022 +0300
added file 2
commit 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225
Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com>
Date: Wed May 25 22:34:23 2022 +0300
our first file

Now let’s use git reset to go back to our first commit using the commit id. At first, we will try it without any flag:

git reset 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225 // this is the first commit id

Now let’s rerun git status:

git status
On branch master
Untracked files:
(use “git add <file>…” to include in what will be committed)
file2.txt

When we ran git reset without any flag git used the mixed flag by default. Meaning the changes from our second commit were removed from the Head and the Index, but still stayed in the working directory. Let’s stage the changes again and commit them:

git add file2.txt
git commit -m “added file 2 again”
[master c211158] added file 2 again
1 file changed, 0 insertions(+), 0 deletions(-)
create mode 100644 file2.txt

Ok. now when we run git log we will see only two commits, the first commit, and the second commit:

git log
commit c211158cee7a04c1cba4c7e10a2474c64b298d1c (HEAD -> master)
Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com>
Date: Wed May 25 23:42:56 2022 +0300
added file 2 again // this is the new commit
commit 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225
Author: Yair <yair543210@gmail.com>
Date: Wed May 25 22:34:23 2022 +0300
our first file

Now we will use git reset, but this time with the hard flag:

git reset — hard 7ed5dc012974aa577184f71077d02ccc63653225
HEAD is now at 7ed5dc0 our first file
git status
On branch master
nothing to commit, working tree clean

When using the “hard” flag we deleted everything that was done after the commit we went back to. Because we created another file after that commit, that file is gone:

There is no file2.txt anymore.

Conclusion

Using git reset can be beneficial when we understand what it does. I highly recommend reading more about it and trying to use it in a safe environment like I just did in this tutorial. I truly believe that this is the best way to learn, by taking little examples.

This article was originally published at Medium
item.author: yairmishnayot

item.title: 我用更快的虚拟 DOM 制作了 React | I made React with a faster Virtual DOM
item.description:
它非常快，并且可以轻松创建用户界面。

哦，伙计……另一个/virtual dom|javascript/gim库？ 我对React已经很好了，为什么还要费心切换呢？

通过使用编译器预先计算用户界面，Million 减少了传统 Virtual DOM 的开销。

好的很酷......但是如果我需要更轻量级的东西，我可以使用Preact ，我为什么要使用 Million？

虽然像Preact这样的替代库通过有效的代码设计减少了包大小，但 Million 通过利用编译在改进包大小和渲染速度方面取得了巨大的飞跃，更进一步。

把它想象成Preact和Svelte生了一个孩子。 速度超快的宝宝！ 使用million/react

这是一个使用 Million 的 Counter 应用程序的极其简单的实现。

 import { compat , createRoot , useState } from ' million/react ' ; function Counter ({ init }) { const [ value , setValue ] = useState ( init ); return ( < div > < div > Counter : { value } < /div > < button onClick = {() => setValue ( value + 1 )} > Increment < /button > < button onClick = {() => setValue ( value - 1 )} > Decrement < /button > < /div > ); } const root = createRoot ( document . querySelector ( ' #app ' )); // Million wraps render functions inside a compat function compat (() => { root . render ( < Counter init = { 0 } /> ) ; });

在这里，您可以编写 React 代码。 打开项目开始修改：

在使用 React 方面需要帮助吗？ 查看React 文档。

这是非常非常早期的阶段，所以要为奇怪的错误/插件不兼容等做好准备。如果您有任何建议，如果您在评论中回复，我会非常高兴！



原文:

Plug: I help develop Million: Virtual DOM into the future! 💥🦁✨

Million is a lightweight (<1kb) Virtual DOM. It's really fast and makes it easy to create user interfaces.

Oh man... Another /virtual dom|javascript/gim library? I'm fine with React already, why should I bother switching?

Million makes creating user interfaces as easy as React, but with faster performance and smaller bundle size for the end user. By computing the user interface beforehand with a compiler, Million reduces the overhead of traditional Virtual DOM.

Okay cool... but why should I use Million if I can just use Preact if I need something a bit more lightweight?

While alternative libraries like Preact reduce bundle sizes by efficient code design, Million takes it a step further by leveraging compilation to make a quantum leap in improving bundle size and render speed.

Think of it as if Preact and Svelte had a baby. A baby with super speed! 👶

Using million/react

Here is an extremely simple implementation of a Counter app using Million.

import { compat, createRoot, useState } from 'million/react'; function Counter({ init }) { const [value, setValue] = useState(init); return ( <div> <div>Counter: {value}</div>
 <button onClick={() => setValue(value + 1)}>Increment</button>
 <button onClick={() => setValue(value - 1)}>Decrement</button>
 </div>
 );
} const root = createRoot(document.querySelector('#app')); // Million wraps render functions inside a compat function
compat(() => { root.render(<Counter init={0} />);
});

Here, you can write React code. Million will automagically optimize it during compile time, allowing for a super speedy Virtual DOM.

Open the project to start tinkering:

Need help on using React? Check out the React documentation.

This is very, VERY early stage, so be prepared for weird bugs / plugin incompatibility / etc. If you have any suggestions, I'd be more than happy if you replied in a comment with it!

item.title: 如何在印度选择合适的软件开发公司？ | How to choose the right software development company in India?
item.description:
软件开发领域被认为是广阔且高度繁荣的工作领域之一，因为几乎每个人或企业主都与它有某种联系。 此外，这是您在印度找到许多软件咨询服务的唯一原因，他们可以在任何与软件开发相关的工作中为您提供帮助。

但是印度如此庞大的软件开发公司却导致了一个大问题，那就是用户无法选择最适合自己的选项。 如果您是这些人中的一员，请务必关注这篇文章，因为在这里我们将看看一些最有用的技巧，这些技巧将帮助您找出最适合您的软件开发公司选项.

选择合适的印度软件咨询公司的提示：

以下是一些提示列表，您可以借助这些提示在印度选择合适的软件咨询公司：

在印度的软件开发公司中，您首先需要寻找的是它的技术可用性。 在此您需要查看其技术团队以及他们在团队中拥有什么样的专业知识，据此您将能够知道您可以从该公司获得什么样的服务。

您应该联系那些使用最新技术和软件集成提供服务的软件开发公司。

始终听取软件开发公司的业务政策，然后再决定是否与他们联系。 确保您从具有透明工作政策的公司那里获得服务，因为在此类业务政策的帮助下，您将了解您的项目正在发生的事情。

不仅如此，公司的政策也会影响到整体工作。 所以看印度软件开发公司的工作政策是很重要的。

我们已经讨论了一些关于团队的问题，但在这里我们再次建议您查看软件开发公司的团队。 因为他们是要为您的项目工作的人，最终结果仅取决于他们的能力。

看看他们的技能，他们是否能够提供

在与印度的一家软件开发公司完成项目时，请倾听他们的业务逻辑以及他们将如何满足您的需求和项目要求的想法。

此外，通过此步骤，您将能够与公司彻底分享您对产品的想法。 因此，他们可以在没有任何混乱的情况下工作。

查看印度软件开发公司的评论是最重要的一步。 在评论的帮助下，您将能够查看公司以前的用户和客户的工作经验。



原文:

The software development field is considered one of the vast and highly flourished working sectors because almost every person or business owner has some link with it. Moreover, this is the only reason you will find many software consulting services in India who are there to help you out in any software development related works.

But this vast number of software development companies in India are leading to one big problem, which is, users are unable to select the most appropriate option for themselves. In case if you are one of those people, then do follow this post till the end, as here we are going to have a look at some of the most useful tips that will help you to figure out the best software development company option for you.

Tips for choosing the right software consulting company India:

Here is the list of few tips with the help of which you can choose the right software consulting company in India:

● Technical availability:

The first thing you need to look for in India's software development company is its technical availability. Under which you need to check out its technical team and what sort of expertise they have in their team, based on which you would be able to know what kind of services you can get from that company.

You should get in touch with those software development companies that provide services using the latest technology and software integrations.

● Work policies:

Always listen to the business policy of a software development company, and after that, make your decision on whether to get in touch with them or not. Make sure you get services from those companies who have transparent work policies, as, with the help of such business policies, you would be aware of what's happening regarding your project.

Not only that, a company’s policy also affects the overall work. So it is an important thing to look at the work policies of software development companies in India.

● Team:

We have already discussed a bit about the team, but here we are again suggesting you to look at the team a software development company has. Because they are the ones who are going to work on your project, and the final outcome depends upon their abilities only.

Have a look at their skills, and are they able to provide an

● Business logic:

During finalizing your project with a software development company in India, listen to their business logic and idea of how they would fulfill your demands and project requirements.

Moreover, with this step, you would be able to share your idea regarding the product with the company thoroughly. Hence they can work on it without any confusion.

● Reviews:

Having a look at the reviews of a software development company in India is the most important step. With the help of reviews, you would be able to look at the work experiences of a company's previous users and clients.
