item.title: 如何聘请精通 React JS 开发人员 | How to Hire Proficient React JS Developer
item.description:
斯瓦拉里·加鲁德

发表于聘请精通 React JS 开发人员
你肯定会为你的下一个项目雇佣 ReactJS 开发人员。 然而，识别杰出人才可能是一项具有挑战性的任务。 在寻找合适的候选人时，需要了解许多因素，例如足够的经验、技术专长、软技能等等。

该博客提供了有关如何雇用 React 开发人员的全面路线图，以及反映招聘过程之前和期间活动的各个方面。

专业 React JS 开发人员的关键职责

使用 React 工具包构建交互式 UI 组件和元素。 构建和优化可重用的 HTML/CSS/JavaScript 元素并使它们交叉兼容。 持续跟踪代码以推进和增强应用程序性能。 利用 JavaScript 样式表和功能编写精确、富有表现力的现代用户界面代码。 处理应用程序的库以启用异步 API 调用并使用上下文 API 和 Redux 工作流生成计算数据。

检查 QA 流程，包括综合测试、单元测试和集成测试。

你如何着手和雇用 ReactJS 开发人员？

了解您正在探索的经验水平和技能
您应该考虑聘请专门的 ReactJS 开发人员，他们牢牢掌握核心技术概念和依赖于您的项目需求的经验。 这主要是等式的硬技能方面。

聘请精通 React 开发人员时需要探索的硬技能： 精通JavaScript 精通 React 假设 精通 React JS 使用过 iOS 和 Android 功能

了解重要的技术工具

当您聘请熟练的 React 开发人员时需要探索的软技能：
开发人员必须具备良好的沟通技巧并具有团队精神。 不仅要聘请 React JS 专家，他们还应该是不拘泥于传统方法的创造性和问题解决者。

在正确的地方寻找专业人士
您可以发布 React JS 招聘要求并获得候选人的一些优秀网站是：

工作门户（Naukri、Monster 和其他）LinkedIn Stack Overflow Toptal CodementorX X-Team BrainHub Upwork

您可以浏览 Clutch 和 GoodFirms 等目录，了解领先的 React JS 应用程序开发公司。

创建明确的职位描述
如果您创建职位描述的方法能够在寻找具有挑战性角色的优秀程序员眼中将您定位为寻找的雇主，这将有所帮助。

您甚至可以使用您的职位描述，通过表明您对卓越品质的选择来吓跑不合适的候选人。 您可以声明您只想要具有特定经验或在特定领域工作的候选人。

提出精确的问题
在这里，我们汇总了一些你可以在面试中提出的精确问题，以便从合适的选择中选出最好的一批 React JS 开发人员：

你认为 React JS 的局限是什么？ 你能描述一下 React JS 组件的生命周期吗？ 你将如何提高 React 应用程序的生产力？

选择合适的候选人

合适的申请人不需要精通你所做的一切。 但是，他们必须表现出更高水平的兴趣和学习意愿。 只有对您的项目充满热情的候选人才能为您当前的团队增加价值。

雇用熟练的 React 开发人员时要检查的六件事

  1. 检查 JavaScript 熟练程度

对于开发人员来说，理解 JavaScript 作用域、事件冒泡、原型继承、DOM 操作和其他关键功能甚至意义重大。

  1. 对 React JS 概念的认识

由于 React JS 是一个具有可重用元素的广泛生态系统，因此您需要一个熟悉所有概念的经验丰富的开发人员。

熟悉 React 元素、样式库和 CSS 策略可帮助开发人员创建响应式用户界面和制作丰富的 UI。 您选择的开发人员必须了解 CSS 技术，例如 CSS 预处理器、JSS、CSS-in-JS 和 CSS 框架。

因此，当您聘请能够启用编程并执行样式部分的 ReactJS 开发人员时，将获得巨大的好处。

  1. 交叉检查相邻技术的知识

您选择的 React JS 开发人员必须具有使用与 React 完美匹配的相邻技术的经验。 因此，熟悉这些技术可以积极地为任何开发人员提供优越的优势。

除此之外，人们应该对 MERN Stack（包括 Express JS、MongoDB、React JS 和 Node JS）等技术有很好的掌握，以便在 React JS 开发项目中蓬勃发展。

  1. 检查他们对测试工具的理解

当你雇佣 ReactJS 开发人员时，检查他们对测试工具的理解。 检查版本控制系统的技能

当您正在执行一个多方面的软件项目时，请考虑一个实例，该项目需要更多的开发人员相互合作并经常对代码进行更改。 在这种情况下，版本控制系统使开发过程现代化并协助代码管理。

这就是为什么应该优先考虑使用版本控制系统的 React JS 开发人员。

  1. 他们有灵活的学习曲线吗？

由于其详尽的 React 概念、库和语法文档，JavaScript 开发人员无需花费太多时间来学习。

伊曼纽尔·艾耶尼格巴 - May 26 5月26日



原文:

Swarali Garud

Posted on Jun 1

Hire Proficient React JS Developers
You are definite to hire ReactJS developers for your next project. However, identifying exceptional talent can be a challenging task. There are many factors to comprehend while looking for the right candidate, such as sufficient experience, technology expertise, soft skills, and much more.

This blog offers a comprehensive roadmap on how to hire React developer, together with facets to reflect on activities before and while the hiring process.

**

Critical Responsibilities of Professional React JS Developers

** Create higher quality user interface code leveraging wireframes and mock designs. Build interactive UI components and elements using React toolkit. Build and optimize reusable HTML/CSS/JavaScript elements and turn them cross-compatible. Track the code continuously to advance and enhance app performance. Write precise, expressive, and modern-day user interface code leveraging the JavaScript stylesheets and features. Deal with app’s libraries to enable asynchronous API calls and originate computed data using Context APIs and Redux workflows.

Check QA processes comprising comprehensive testing, unit testing, and integration testing.

How do you go about and Hire ReactJS Developers?

Know the Experience Level and Skills you are Exploring
You should look to hire dedicated ReactJS developers who have a firm grip on the core technical concepts and experience reliant on your project needs. That is primarily the hard skills facet of the equation.

Hard skills to explore when you hire proficient react developers: A brilliant acquaintance of JavaScript Expertise in React assumptions Proficiency with React JS Worked on iOS and Android functionalities

Understanding of significant technology tools

Soft skills to explore when you hire proficient react developers:
The developer must have good communications skills and be a team player. Not merely hire React JS experts, but they should also be creative and problem solvers who don’t limit themselves to the traditional approaches.

Look for Professionals in the Correct Places
Some of the excellent sites where you can post your React JS hiring requirements and get candidates are:

Job Portals (Naukri, Monster and others) LinkedIn Stack Overflow Toptal CodementorX X-Team BrainHub Upwork

You could explore directories such as Clutch and GoodFirms to check out the leading React JS application development companies.

Create Explicit Job Description
It will help if you create the job description with an approach that spots you as a looked-for employer in the eyes of brilliant programmers searching for challenging roles.

You can even use your job description to frighten away inappropriate candidates by signifying your choice for superior quality. You can state that you only want candidates with specific experience or worked in a particular domain.

Ask the Precise Questions
Here, we have a roundup of some precise questions you could ask in the interview to single out the best batch of React JS developers out of the apt choices:

What is React JS development utilized for? What are the significant strengths of React JS technology? What do you think are the limits of React JS? Can you describe the lifecycle of a React JS component? How would you improve the productivity of a React app?

Choose the Right Candidate

The right applicant doesn’t need to be proficient at everything you do. However, they must showcase a higher level of interest and readiness to learn. Only the candidate who is enthusiastic about working on your project would add worth to your current team.

Six Things to Check While You Hire Proficient React Developers

  1. Check Out JavaScript Proficiency

React is a precise JavaScript library, and so a React JS developer needs to have a powerful grip over JavaScript functionalities, syntax, and operations. It is even significant for developers to comprehend the JavaScript scopes, event bubbling, prototypal inheritance, DOM manipulation and other crucial functions.

  1. Awareness of React JS Concepts

Since React JS is an extensive ecosystem with reusable elements, you require a practised developer aware of all concepts.

An acquaintance of React elements, styling libraries, and CSS tactics assists developers in creating responsive user interfaces and crafting rich UIs. Your selected developers must know CSS techniques such as CSS Pre-processors, JSS, CSS-in-JS and CSS Frameworks.

So, when you hire ReactJS developers who can enable programming and do the styling part is an immense benefit.

  1. Cross Check Knowledge of Adjacent Technologies

React JS developers you select must have experience working with adjacent technologies that are well-matched with React. Laravel, Ruby on Rails and Django can work in synch with React. Hence, familiarity with these technologies positively offers a superior edge to any developer.

Apart from this, one should have an excellent grip over technology such as MERN Stack (which includes Express JS, MongoDB, React JS, and Node JS) to flourish with React JS development projects.

  1. Check their understanding of testing tools

When you hire ReactJS developers, check their understanding of testing tools. Some of the ideal and most used tools with ReactJS are:

Jest, Enzyme, Mocha, AVA, Tape, Chai and Karma (For unit and integration testing)
Selenium, Cypress, Cucumber.js, Puppeteer, and Nightwatch.js (For comprehensive testing)

  1. Check Skills of Version Control Systems

Think of an instance when you are executing a multifaceted software project that needs more developers working mutually and making recurrent alterations in the code. In such scenarios, the version control system modernizes the development procedure and assists with code management.

That is why React JS developers with practice in working with version control systems should be given the foremost preference.

  1. Do they have Supple Learning Curve?

React JS is built on JavaScript, so the learning strain is pretty modest. Thanks to its exhaustive documentation of React concepts, libraries, and syntax, that won’t take much time to learn for JavaScript developers.

Emmanuel Aiyenigba - May 26
item.author: garudswarali

item.title: 我的 HTML 基础 | MY HTML BASICS
item.description:
武卡尼·格卡巴什

发表于编写 HTML 代码的结构是使用标签告诉浏览器它正在为用户解释什么。

创建 HTML 文件：

创建 html 文件意味着创建一个扩展名为.html的文件。

基本标签：

  • 标题标签用于为代表该页面的窗口或选项卡命名。
  • 身体标签属性示例：

    • 将背景颜色设置为body标签的绿色。 您可以使用颜色名称或十六进制值

    <body bgcolor="green"> ... </body>

    • 使用名称或十六进制值设置链接的文本颜色：

    <body link="green"> ... </body>

    • 使用名称或十六进制值设置访问链接的颜色：

    <body alink="green"> ... </body>

    文字标签：

    这些标记用于格式化您添加到 html 的文本内容，并且您使用以下 HTML 标记。

    • 创建预格式化的文本。

    <pre> ... </pre>

    • 创建标题标签 h1、h2...h6 其中 h1-largest 和 h6-smallest：

    <h1> Heading Text </h1>

    <b> test to turn bold </b>

    • 显示/定义源代码通常是等宽的：

    <code> ... </code>

    • 强调单词或文本：

    <strong> ... </strong>

    • 设置字体大小，替换“？” 以 em、rem、% 或 px 为单位的尺寸作为度量：

    <font size="?"> ... </font>

    <body> ... </body>

    格式化：

    我们使用这些方法对某些内容类型（如文章）、容器（如 div 跨度）进行分组。

    容器标签：

    这些标签用于在其中包含其他标签或内容，您可以这样做以创建一些分段，以便能够共享属性“样式、格式”或您打算将数据分段的其他原因。

    • 我们通常在大多数时候使用 div 标签<div>...</div>并且在这个标签中我们添加的内容将是多行的部分。

    • 我们还使用 span 标签在 div 中创建部分，也使用<span> ... </span>并用于使用 CSS 格式化内联内容。

    • 然后，我们使用<p>... </p>将该段落标记中的内容作为一个段落包含在内。

    这是我们的基本输入标签，其中 type 属性的值可以是email 、 password 、 number 、 url或文本“我认为还有更多”，但这是我使用的那些，但这些用于验证您的输入以及帮助读者理解他们期望提供给网页的数据。

  • 我遇到的另一个包括<input type='date/month/week/time name='date' />这一个用于输入日期。

  • 另一个很好的是<input type='search' name='search' />用于搜索。

  • 我喜欢的另一个有趣的是<input type="color" value="green" />

  • 我们还具有name属性，可帮助您在多个输入的情况下识别具有 name 属性的输入标签。

  • 然后我们还有value属性，它被大量用于捕获您放入 input 标签的输入。

  • 我们还有disabled属性，它确定输入是否可以在添加数据方面访问。

所以这就是我有时间写出的 HTML 的基础知识，但肯定有更多的标签，你的工作是找出它们并利用它们来使网站变得用户友好和可爱。

我希望这对您有所帮助，这也是 HTML文档的另一个更详细信息来源，请使用它，并查看我的视频和内容，了解如何使用 python 示例使用文档。



原文:

Vukani Gcabashe

Posted on Jun 1

The structure for writing HTML code is using tags to tell the browser what it's interpreting for you the user.

Creating HTML File:

Creating the html file means creating a file with the .html extension.

Basic Tags:

  • html tag is the one that will initialize / format the HTML document for the browser. 
    <html>

        // Your other tags will go here

    </html/>
  • head tag is where we put tags and meta-tags that will describe the html document.


    • the head tag goes inside the <html></html> tag but its one tag who's content you won't see displayed on the html, but perhaps the window.

        <head> 
        // code for other tags and meta-tags goes here
    </head>
    • title tag is used to give a name to the window or tab that will represent that page.
    • body tag is used to contain the web pages content, this is where you put all content.
    
        //web page content goes here

    Body Tag Attributes Examples:

    • to set the background-color to green of body tag. You can use color name or hex value

    <body bgcolor="green"> ... </body>

    • to set text color of links, using name or hex value:

    <body link="green"> ... </body>

    • to set color of visited links, using name or hex value:

    <body alink="green"> ... </body>

    Text Tags:

    These tags are used to format text content you adding to your html and you use the following HTML tags.

    • to create pre-formatted text.

    <pre> ... </pre>

    • to create header tags h1, h2...h6 where the h1-largest & h6-smallest:

    <h1> Heading Text </h1>

    <b> test to turn bold </b>

    • to display / define source code usually monospace:

    <code> ... </code>

    • to emphasize a word or text:

    <strong> ... </strong>

    • to set the size of the font, replace "?" with size in em, rem, % or px as measurement:

    <font size="?"> ... </font>

    <body> ... </body>

    Formatting:

    We use these methods to group certain content types like articles, container like div spans.

    Container tags:

    these tags are used to contain other tags or content within them, you would do this to create some sectioning, to be able to share attributes "style, format" or other reasons you intend for data to be sectioned out.

    • We commonly use div tag most times <div>...</div> and within this tag we add content that will be sections with many lines.

    • We also use the span tag to create sections within divs also <span> ... </span> and its used to format inline content with CSS.

    • We then have the <p>... </p> to contain content within this paragraph tag as one paragraph.

    Html Input Tags:

    <input type="email" name=? value=? disabled=? />

    • This is our basic input tag where the type attribute will have a value of either email, password, number , url or text "i think there is more" but that's the ones i have used, but these are used to validate your input and also helps the reader understand the data they are expected to give the web page.

    • Another that i have encountered include <input type='date/month/week/time name='date' /> and this one is used to input to date.

    • Another good one is <input type='search' name='search' /> that is used for search.

    • Another fun one i loved was <input type="color" value="green" />

    • We also have the name attribute that will help you identify the input tag with the name attribute in situations with more than one input.

    • Then we also have the value attribute that is used a lot to capture the input that you have put into the input tag.

    • We also have the disabled attribute which determines if the input is accessible in terms of adding data.

    So that's the basics of HTML i got time to write out but there are definitely more tags out there and your job is to seek them out and use them to your advantage to make the site user friendly and lovable.

    I hope this helped you out and this is also another more details source for the documentation for HTML please use it and also check out my video & content on how to use documentation using python example.
item.author: nash4253

item.title: 编码和多动症 - 无法开始 | Coding and ADHD - Can't Start
item.description:
我想开始任务。 我知道我需要开始任务。 为什么我不能开始任务？

耻辱

多动症的大脑收到了太多关于他们完成任务能力的负面信息，以至于无法开始往往源于羞耻。 我们中的许多人都在与完美主义作斗争，没有人喜欢在同一任务上反复尝试和失败。

我强烈推荐 How to ADHD 和 Brendan Mahan 的可怕解释之墙第 1部分和第 2 部分。 我发现非常有用的一本短书是如何在溺水时保持房屋。 我发布了关于我的 ADHD 的推文并关注了许多关于他们的 ADHD 的推文，因为知道其他人也在与这些事情作斗争是非常有帮助的。

压倒

多动症患者经常发现配对/共同工作/身体加倍有帮助，尤其是在开始一项任务时。 我必须找到一个平衡点，因为我会在有机会的时候偷听别人的耳朵，这不利于完成我的工作。

在我的一生中，当我遇到一个我不想开始的大任务时，对我有用的主要事情就是写下所有的步骤。 我经常发现我对某件事感到不知所措，因为当我实际上是 20 岁时，我认为它是一个步骤。

接下来，我将每个步骤分解成最小的步骤。 然后我在该列表中找到最小的步骤并尝试它。 对于代码，有时这就像制作一个空白文件并导入一些东西一样简单。 一旦我完成它，我的大脑就会宣布任务开始，突然间事情变得不那么压倒性了。

这对于患有多动症的人来说可能非常困难，所以永远不要害怕寻求帮助来打破它。 这可能是开发工作适合多动症患者的一个领域。 讨论任务中涉及的各个步骤以正确估计它们并确定它们的优先级是正常的。 通常会有一个人的工作是帮助您这样做，如果没有，与其他开发人员配对可能是一件好事。 如果您的公司内没有这样的开发人员，您可以请朋友或加入网络组。

一点点

我最近学到的一点是，投入大量时间尝试这项任务或一小部分任务确实对 ADHD 大脑有效。 或者“我只做这个重构的 5%”通常足以让我们开始。 一旦这 5% 完成，这种势头通常会提供足够的动力来开始另一个 5%，依此类推。

在一天工作结束时，我经常会问自己是否有一些小任务我现在可以做，以使早上更容易开始。 对我来说，这通常是为了确保我明天的待办事项清单上有我想在早上尝试的第一个任务，以及我所有的会议/约会/活动。 有时，它会整理办公桌上的杂物以限制分心，或者在帖子上给自己写一个鼓励性的便条。

在我坐在沙发上放松之前，我试着问自己明天我能做些什么小任务来帮助自己正常工作。 例如，我是否可以在 3 步或更少的时间内准备好早餐？ 如果没有，我应该准备一些。 我正在努力寻找在我有动力的时候能做的最小的任务，而不是希望我早上有动力。

结论

我错过了您喜欢的资源或提示吗？ 发表评论！

快来了：

  • 无法继续
  • 停不下来
  • 不记得


原文:

I want to start the task. I know I need to start the task. Why can't I start the task?

Shame

ADHD brains have received so much negative messaging about their ability to do tasks, that inability to start is often rooted in shame. Many of us struggle with perfectionism, and no one enjoys trying and failing at the same task repeatedly.

I highly recommend How to ADHD and Brendan Mahan's Wall of Awful explanation part 1 and part 2. A short book I found very helpful is How to Keep House While Drowning. Jesse J. Anderson also does a great job of explaining how ADHD brains are motivated and some tools to use in this video. Black Girl Lost Keys has tons of helpful resources. I tweet about my ADHD and follow many people who tweet about their ADHD because it is very helpful to know other people are struggling with these things too.

Overwhelm

ADHD people often find pairing/co-working/body doubling helpful, especially for starting a task. I have to find a balance because I will chat someone's ear off given the opportunity, to the detriment of getting my work done.

Throughout my life, the main thing that has worked for me when I encounter a large task I don't want to start is writing down all the steps. Often I find I'm overwhelmed by something because I'm thinking of it as one step when it's actually 20.

Next I break each of those steps down into its smallest steps. Then I find the smallest step in that list and try it. For code, sometimes this is as easy as making a blank file and importing something. Once I've done it, my brain declares the task started, and suddenly things are much less overwhelming.

This can be very difficult for people with ADHD, so never be afraid to ask for help breaking it down. This can be one area in which development work suits people with ADHD. It is normal to discuss the individual steps involved in a task in order to properly estimate and prioritize them. There is often a person whose job it is to help you do so, and if not, it could be a great thing to pair with another developer on. If there's not a developer like that within your company, you can ask a friend or join a networking group.

Just a Little Bit

Something I've learned recently is that committing to an amount of time trying the task or a small percent of the task really works for ADHD brains. "I'll do 15 minutes of dishes." or "I'll just do 5% of this refactor" is often enough to get us started. Once that 5% is complete, that momentum often gives enough motivation to start another 5% and so on.

At the end of the work day, I'll often ask myself if there are small tasks I can do right now to make it easier to start in the morning. For me, this is usually making sure my to do list for tomorrow has the first task I want to try in the morning and all my meetings/appointments/events on it. Sometimes it's picking up desk clutter to limit distractions or writing myself an encouraging note on a post it.

Before I sit on the couch to relax, I try to ask myself what small task I can do to help myself function tomorrow. For example, do I have breakfast I can prep in 3 steps or less? If not, I should prep some. I'm trying to find the smallest task I can do while I have some momentum, instead of hoping I have the motivation in the morning.

Conclusion

Did I miss a resource or tip you love? Leave a comment!

Coming soon:

  • Can't Keep Going
  • Can't Stop
  • Can't Remember

item.title: 你是谁并不重要。 你能做的是唯一重要的事 | It doesn't matter who you are. What you can do is the only thing that matters
item.description:
陶克里斯托弗·高桥

发表于有没有人年纪轻轻就进入软件行业？？？ 我有。 我16岁进入软件行业，已经两年了。 我经历了很多挑战和困难。 年龄歧视不仅针对老年人，还针对年轻人（又名反向年龄歧视），尤其是在我生活的地方，年龄就是一切。 尽管如此，我一直在努力申请我能申请的每一家公司，并且得到了回报。 我终于有了一个相对稳定的职业和适合我的年龄和国家的好薪水。 学校对我来说并不是一条真正的道路，我全心全意地讨厌它。 我喜欢学习和研究，但被关在一个没有任何自由或没有机会做出自己决定的房间里，真的让我非常沮丧。 软件工程的美妙之处在于没有人关心你来自哪里或你是谁。 很多招聘经理都有一种“如果你能建立你就被录用”的心态，我因此得救了。 这个故事的寓意是，如果你努力工作并保持一致，那么你来自哪里或你是谁都无关紧要。 我来自一个单身母亲家庭，无论如何并不富有，但我纯粹的意志和为我想要的东西工作的能力让我来到了这里。 所以无论谁在读这篇文章，我希望这能给你所追求的一切带来希望。 我支持你。



原文:

Tao Christopher Takahashi

Posted on Jun 1

Has anyone entered the software industry at a young age??? I have. I entered the software industry at 16 and its been two years. There were many challenges and hardships I've gone through. Ageism isn't only targeted at old people but young people too(aka. reverse ageism), especially where I live age is everything. Despite that I kept working hard applying to every company I can and it paid off. I finally have a relatively secure career and a good salary for my age and country. School wasn't really a path for me I hated it with all my heart. I love learning and researching but being locked up in a room without any freedom or the chance to make my own decisions really gave me severe depression. The wonderful thing about software engineering is no one cares where you came for or who you are. A lot of hiring managers have a "IF YOU CAN BUILD YOU'RE HIRED" mentality and I was saved by that. Moral of the story is that if you work hard and stay consistent it wont matter where you came from or who you are. Im from a single mother household and was not wealthy in anyway but my sheer will and ability to work for what I want got me here. So whoever is reading this, I hope this gave you hope in whatever you are pursuing. Im rooting for you.
item.author: criscmd

item.title: 支持 Android 上的 RTL 设计 | Supporting RTL Design on Android
item.description:
你好安卓追随者。 今天我将在 Android 上写下我所有的 RTL（从右到左）支持设计经验。 我希望这篇文章能节省你的时间。 拿上你的茶/咖啡/水，让我们开始吧。


世界上几乎所有的剧本都是从左到右写的。 但是一些像阿拉伯语这样的脚本是从右向左书写的。 所以为了最好的用户体验，我们也应该支持从右到左的设计。

当 UI 从 LTR 更改为 RTL（反之亦然）时，它被命名为“镜像”。 如果您正在寻找关于哪些 UI 组件应该镜像以及哪些 UI 组件不应该镜像的设计指南，您可以查看material.io 。

不应镜像时钟和电话号码等数字。

  • 例如，如果 LTR 中的数字是 123456，那么 RTL 中的数字也必须是 123456。 不要反转它。 此类文本的方向必须是 LTR。
  • 不应该镜像未翻译成 RTL 语言的文本（即使它是短语的一部分）
  • 图表和图形不应该被镜像。
  • 不传达方向的图标，例如相机📸、搜索🔍、键盘⌨️、耳机🎧、通话📞，不应镜像
  • 包含数字的图标应该针对使用不同数字的其他语言进行本地化。
  • 您不应该在图像上镜像斜线。 因为 LTR 斜线描述了 LTR 和 RTL 语言的关闭状态。 例如禁止停车
  • 如果媒体播放按钮和媒体进度指示器指的是正在播放的磁带的方向，则不应镜像它们

    • 记住。

    首先，您必须将 android:supportsRtl="true" 添加到您的应用程序清单文件中的元素以支持 RTL 设计。

    技巧：如果您的应用程序支持多种语言，并且如果您有像config.setLayoutDirection(Locale.US)这样的代码片段，则必须更改它。 否则，您的应用将不支持 RTL 设计。 您可以更改代码行，如下所示。


    将布局方向设置为语言环境基础

    之后，将应用程序的所有左/右布局属性 start/end 替换为其等效项。 如果您必须支持早于 SDK 17 的版本，则应在同一视图中同时使用 left/right 和 start/end 属性。 当您执行此操作时，Android 操作系统将自动处理您几乎所有的视图布局镜像。 在我看来，将你的 minSdkVersion 设置为 21 所以 Android 5。因为 2021 年几乎所有手机的 sdk 版本都超过 21。在本文中，我假设你的 minSdkVersion 为 19 或更高。

    当您考虑将所有左/右属性替换为开始/结束时，您会感到很糟糕。 停止这种感觉，冷静下来，继续阅读。 我有一个窍门给你。

    技巧：Android Studio 只是有一个工具，可以用它们的 RTL 等值替换所有属性。 为此，请按照以下步骤操作。

    在 res 文件夹上单击右键。 之后按住 Refactor 上的光标，最后单击 Add Right-to-Left (RTL) Support。 完成这些操作后，将出现一个窗口，如下所示。

    在此窗口中，取消标记 Update AndroidManifest.xml 并标记 Replace Left/Right Properties with Start/End properties 并运行它。 操作完成后会出现如下窗口。

    它向您展示了哪些布局将被重构。 如果您只想重构您的项目布局，您可以从第三方库中排除其他布局文件。

    选择您不想重构的布局文件，单击右键并排除所有。 完成这些后，您的布局文件将如下所示。

    之后单击“重构”。 现在您的所有布局属性都更改为它们的等价物。 检查您的本地更改并运行项目💨。 您可以看到您的几乎所有布局都将支持 RTL 设计。

    仅通过这些更改，您的应用程序可能无法完全支持 RTL 设计。 对于完全受支持的 RTL 设计，您应该注意以下部分。

    支持定向资源

    因此，如果我们有定向图像，我们必须手动反转它。

    您知道 android 项目资源目录有一些限定符，例如-night 、 -v24 、 -v21 、 -xxhdpi 、 -ar 。 像这些一样，还有 -ldrtl 限定符，意思是“从右到左的布局方向”，用于处理 RTL 语言的资源。


    支持图像、图标


    定向图像/图标

    假设我们有一个像上面的 A 选项一样的购物车图像。 它必须在 RTL 设计中显示为 B 选项。 通常我们将图像作为res/drawable/ic_cart.png或res/drawable/ic_cart.xml放在我们的项目中。 为了在 RTL 设计中支持此图像，首先准备一个名为res/drawable -ldrtl的新目录。 之后，获取与 LTR 图像同名的从右到左受支持的图像（B 选项），并将其放在可绘制的-ldrtl目录中。 加载界面时，Android 操作系统将呈现正确的文件。 它将同时支持 LTR 设计和 RTL 设计。

    技巧：如果您将图标用作矢量资源（扩展名为 .xml 的文件），则不需要带有 -ldrtl 限定符的新目录。 当您在矢量标签中添加android:autoMirrored="true"行时，您的图标将自动支持 RTL。 这样，您的 apk 大小会更小。 当你想改变你的图标时，只改变一个文件就足够了。

    支持动画

    使用 X 轴的动画在 RTL 设计中效果不佳。 您可以准备 res\transition-ldrtl 或 res\anim-ldrtl 目录，并将 RTL 设计支持的动画文件放置在这些目录中。 确保 LTR 动画文件和 RTL 动画文件名相同。 例如 res\anim\ slide_out_right.xml和 res\anim -ldrtl\slide_out_right.xml

    支持意见

    支持 RecyclerView

    官方支持。 除上述内容外，您无需执行任何操作。 多么美妙，不是吗？

    支持 ViewPager

    如果您在项目中使用 ViewPager2，您也可以跳过本节。 因为 ViewPager2 官方支持 RTL 设计。 否则，如果您使用 ViewPager，您可以通过 2 种方式支持 RTL 设计； 旋转一些视图并迁移到 ViewPager2。

    旋转视图

    您需要将一个新值作为locale_viewpager_rotation= 0添加到 integers.xml。 之后在values-ldrtl文件夹下准备另一个integers.xml文件。 在此 xml 文件中添加locale_viewpager_rotation = 180值。 最后将android:rotationY="@integer/locale_viewpager_rotation"代码片段添加到您的viewpager（您也可以将此方法用于任何视图）。这样，您的viewpager 将在RTL 设计上旋转。为了完全支持可能需要旋转内容浏览器项目也是如此。还有指标。

    我试过这个方法。 这不是一个有效的解决方案，所以我认为只需迁移到 ViewPager 2。

    迁移 ViewPager2

    因此，为了获得最佳解决方案，请将您的 ViewPager 适配器迁移到 ViewPager2适配器。 并获得 ViewPager2 的好处。

    此外，如果您将 TabLayout 与 ViewPager 一起使用，您也必须将其迁移到 ViewPager2 实现。 您可以使用

    代码片段而不是mTab​​Layout.setupWithViewPager(mViewPager) 。

    支持文本视图

    如果您使用起始属性而不是左右属性，则它在 RTL 设计中效果很好。 因此，当您将 RTL 脚本文本设置为 textview 时，文本将自动从右侧开始。

    但是如果在 RTL 模式下你设置了一个 LTR 脚本文本，textView 的文本将像下面这样从左开始（即使你将左/右替换为开始/结束）。

    如果你不想这样做，你可以添加android:textDirection=”locale”属性到你的 textViews。我推荐这个，因为屏幕变得更加流畅。当你这样做时，你的 textViews 如下所示。即使 textView 的文本 LTR 脚本，会从右边开始，当然你可以把这个属性和styles.xml中的样式结合起来。

    最后

    在某些情况下，如果您有义务将 textView 方向设置为 LTR，并且如果您希望在 RTL 设计中显示右侧的 LTR 脚本，您可以设置

    以编程方式。

    支持编辑文本

    如果您使用起始属性而不是左右属性，则它在 RTL 设计中效果很好。 因此，当您将 RTL 脚本文本写入 edittext 时，edittext 的文本将自动从右开始。

    但是当应用程序处于 RTL 模式时，如果您编写 LTR 脚本文本，editText 的文本将像 TextView 一样从左开始。 如果您不想这样做，甚至在 LTR 脚本中，如果您想 edditext 的文本从右侧开始，您可以尝试以下选项

    • 首先，要小心添加android:gravity属性。
    • 如果您的 edittext 在 TextInputLayout 内，您可以将android:textDirection=”locale”属性添加到TextInputLayout 。 它将像下面一样工作


    如果android:textDirection=”locale”不起作用，您可以尝试将android:textAlignment=”viewStart”属性添加到TextInputEditText

    • 如果你有简单的编辑文本，你可以添加android:textDirection=”locale”属性到你的 EditText。 如果android:textDirection=”locale”不起作用，您可以尝试将android:textAlignment=”viewStart”属性添加到 EditText。
    • 如果要将光标固定在编辑文本的最右侧，可以使用下面的代码片段

    支持 WebView

    当您将 HTML 数据/块加载到 webView 时，如果它没有 RTL 支持，您可以通过下面的代码片段对其进行操作。 因此它将支持 RTL 设计。

    支持特定脚本的自定义布局

    如果您想添加专为阿拉伯语文本设计的特定版本的布局，您的目录结构将如下所示：

    一些您可能需要的代码片段

    • 如果您使用Gravity.LEFT/RIGHT ，则应替换为Gravity.START/END 。
    • 如果您想了解视图的布局方向，可以通过view.layoutDirection代码片段获取
    • 要获取上下文的布局方向，您可以使用此上下文。 资源。 配置。 要更改 layoutDirection，您可以使用视图。 布局方向= 查看。 要检查布局方向，您可以使用如下条件
    • 要获取视图的文本方向，您可以使用view来执行此操作。 文本方向。 对于特定语言环境的布局方向，您可以使用TextUtils。 如果您有自定义视图并且在 RTL 属性更改时需要回调，则可以覆盖以下函数

    结论

    对于您的应用程序中完全支持的 RTL 设计，您可以简单地按照以下步骤操作

    • 将应用程序的所有左/右布局属性替换为它们的等效项 start/end。
    • 如果您使用 ViewPager，只需迁移 ViewPager2。
    • 为您的文本视图设置文本方向“区域设置”
    • 在需要在 RTL 设计中镜像的图标中添加 autoMirrored = true。

    感谢您的阅读。 如果觉得文章有用，请拍手👏分享出去，让更多人受益。

    保持联系

    来源

    支持 Layout Mirroring 开发者 Android



    原文:

    Hello Android followers. Today I’ll write all my RTL ( Right to Left ) support design experiences on Android. I hope this article will save your time. Take your tea/coffee/water and let's start.


    RTL Support Design

    Almost all scripts in the world are written from Left to Right. But some script like Arabic is written from Right to Left. So for the best user experience, we should support Right to Left design, too.

    When a UI changed from LTR to RTL (or vice versa) it’s named “ mirroring ”. If you looking for a design guidelines about which UI components should be mirroring and which UI components shouldn't mirroring, you can check out material.io.

    Some Important Points From The material.io Article

    • Numbers such as those on a clock and phone numbers are shouldn’t be mirrored.
    • LTR text shouldn’t be displayed in reverse order. E.g. if a number in LTR is 123456 in RTL must be 123456, too. Don't reverse it. The direction of such texts must be LTR.
    • The texts untranslated to RTL language shouldn’t be mirrored (even if it’s parts of a phrase)
    • Charts and graphs shouldn’t be mirrored.
    • Icons that don’t communicate direction such as a camera 📸, search 🔍, keyboard ⌨️, headset 🎧, call 📞 are shouldn’t be mirrored
    • Icons containing numbers should be localized for other languages that use different numerals.
    • You shouldn’t mirror slashes on images. Because the LTR slashes describe an off state for both LTR and RTL languages. E.g. No Parking
    • Media playback buttons and media progress indicator shouldn't be mirrored if they refer to the direction of tape being played

    The Android OS has native RTL design support since SDK 17 version. Keep in mind.

    First of all, you must add android:supportsRtl="true" to the element in your manifest file for your app to supporting RTL design.

    Trick : If your app supports multiple languages and if you have code snippet like config.setLayoutDirection(Locale.US) you must change it. Otherwise, your app will not support the RTL design. You can change the code line like below.


    Setting layout direction to locale base

    After that, replace your app’s all left/right layout properties start/end with their equivalents. If you must support versions earlier than SDK 17 you should use both left/right and start/end properties in the same view. When you do this The Android OS is gonna handle your almost all views layout mirroring automatically. In my opinion, set your minSdkVersion is 21 so Android 5. Because almost all phones sdk version is over than 21 in 2021. In this article, I assume your minSdkVersion is 19 and higher.

    You can feel terrible when you think about replacing all left/right properties to start/end. Stop feeling like that, calm down and keep reading. I have a trick for you.

    Trick : Android Studio just has a tool for replacing all properties with their RTL equalivents. For doing this follow these steps.

    Click right on res folder. After that hold to the cursor on Refactor finally click Add Right-to-Left (RTL) Support. When you did these a window will appear like below.

    On this window unmark Update AndroidManifest.xml and mark Replace Left/Right Properties with Start/End properties and Run it. After the operations finished a window will appear like below.

    It shows you which layouts will be refactoring. If you want to refactor only your project layouts you can exclude other layout files from came 3rd party libraries.

    Select layout files which you don’t want to refactor, click right and exclude all. When you did these your layout files will be like below.

    After that click Do Refactor. And now your all layout properties changed with their equivalents. Check your local changes and Run Project💨. You can see your almost all layouts will be supporting RTL design.

    By only these changes your app maybe can’t fully support RTL design. For a fully supported RTL design, you should pay attention to the below sections.

    Supporting Directional Resources

    Android’s RTL engine reverses layout direction but doesn’t reverse resources (so drawables) automatically. So if we have a directional image we must reverse it manually.

    You know android projects resources directories have some qualifiers as -night , -v24 , -v21 , -xxhdpi , -ar . Like these also has -ldrtl qualifier meaning “layout direction right-to-left” for resources working on RTL languages.


    Some resources qualifiers on the android project

    Supporting Images, Icons


    Directional image / icon

    Suppose we have a shopping cart image like the above A option. It must be shown as the B option in RTL design. Normally we place A image as res/drawable/ic_cart.png or res/drawable/ic_cart.xml on our project. For supporting this image on RTL design firstly prepare a new directory named as res/drawable -ldrtl. After that get your right to left supported image (B option) having the same name as LTR image and place it in the drawable -ldrtl directory. The Android OS will render the right file when loading your interfaces. It will support LTR design and RTL design both.

    (Also for using some resource files in LTR design you can prepare files with -ldrtl qualifier)

    Trick : If you are using icons as Vector Assets (files with .xml extension) you don’t need a new directory with -ldrtl qualifier. When you add android:autoMirrored="true" line in vector tag, your icon gonna has RTL support automatically. ( This work SDK 19 and higher version. ) So you don’t need to store 2 files in your project. In this way, your apk size will be smaller. And when you want to change your icon it will be enough to change only one file.

    Supporting Animations

    Animations that use X-axis don't work well on RTL design. You can prepare res\transition-ldrtl or res\anim-ldrtl directories and placing RTL design-supported animation files in these directories. Make sure LTR animation file and RTL animation file names are the same. For example, res\anim\ slide_out_right.xml and res\anim -ldrtl\slide_out_right.xml

    Supporting Views

    Supporting RecyclerView

    Officially supported. You don’t need to do anything in addition to the above. How wonderful, isn’t it?

    Supporting ViewPagers

    If you use ViewPager2 in your project also you can skip this section. Because ViewPager2 officially supports RTL design. Otherwise, If you use ViewPager you can support RTL design in 2 ways; rotating some views and migrating to ViewPager2.

    Rotating Views

    You need to add a new value as locale_viewpager_rotation= 0 to integers.xml. After that prepare another integers.xml file under the values-ldrtl folder. Add locale_viewpager_rotation = 180 value in this xml file. Finally android:rotationY="@integer/locale_viewpager_rotation” code snippet to your viewpager (You can also use this method for any view). In this way, your viewpagers will be rotated on RTL design. For fully supporting may need to rotate the content of viewpager items, too. And indicators, too.

    I tried this method. It isn’t an efficient solution so in my opinion just migrate to ViewPager 2.

    Migrating ViewPager2

    ViewPager2 built on RecyclerView so it has RTL support officially. So for the best solution migrate your ViewPager adapter to ViewPager2adapter. And get benefits of ViewPager2.

    Also if you use TabLayout with ViewPager you must migrate it to ViewPager2 implementation, too. You can use

    code snippet instead of mTabLayout.setupWithViewPager(mViewPager) .

    Supporting TextViews

    TextView officially supports RTL design. If you use start-end properties instead of left-right it works well in RTL design. So when you setted an RTL script text to textview, text will start from right automatically.

    But if in RTL mode you set an LTR script text, textView’s text will start left like below (Even if you replaced left/right to start/end).

    If you don't want to this, you can add android:textDirection=”locale" attribute to your textViews. I recommend this because the screen becomes more fluid. When you did this your textViews shown as below. Even if textView’s text LTR script, it will start from right. Of course, you can combine this attribute with styles in styles.xml.

    Finally

    In some cases, if you be obligated to setting textView direction to LTR and if you want to LTR scripts shown on right in RTL design, you can set

    programmatically.

    Supporting EditTexts

    EditText officially supports RTL design. If you use start-end properties instead of left-right it works well in RTL design. So when you write an RTL script text to edittext, the edittext’s text will start from right automatically.

    But when the app is in RTL mode if you write an LTR script text, editText’s text will start left like as TextView. If you don’t want to this and even in LTR scripts if you want to edditext’s text start from right you can try below options

    • First of all, be careful for adding android:gravity property.
    • If your edittext is inside TextInputLayout you can add android:textDirection=”locale” attribute to TextInputLayout. It will works like below


    An EditText inside TextInputLayout

    If android:textDirection=”locale” not works you can try adding android:textAlignment=”viewStart" attribute to TextInputEditText

    • If you have simple edittext you can add android:textDirection=”locale” attribute to your EditText. If android:textDirection=”locale” not works you can try adding android:textAlignment=”viewStart” attribute to EditText.
    • If you want to fix the cursor to the far right of the edittext you can use below code snippet

    Supporting WebViews

    When you loading an HTML data/block to webView if it hasn’t got RTL support you can manipulate it by below code snippet. So it will support RTL design.

    Supporting Custom Layouts for Specific Scripts

    If you want to add a specific version of the layout that is designed for only Arabic text, your directory structure becomes the following:

    res/layout/main.xml This layout file is loaded by default.

    res/layout-ar/main.xml This layout file is loaded with Arabic text.

    res/layout-ldrtl/main.xml This layout file is loaded only for non-Arabic languages that use an RTL text direction.

    Some Code Snippets which maybe you’ll need

    • If you use Gravity.LEFT/RIGHT you should replace to Gravity.START/END.
    • If you want to learn the layout direction of a view you can obtain it by view.layoutDirection code snippet
    • For obtaining the layout direction of a context you can use this context.resources.configuration.layoutDirection code snippet.
    • For changing layoutDirection you can use view.layoutDirection = View.LAYOUT_DIRECTION_RTL code snippet
    • For checking layout direction is you can use a condition like below
    • For obtaining text direction of a view you can do this with view.textDirection. S_o you can set like _view.textDirection = view.textDirection = View.TEXT_DIRECTION_RTL
    • For layout direction for specific locale you can use TextUtils. getLayoutDirectionFromLocale (locale)
    • If you have a custom view and if you need a callback when RTL properties changed you can override the below function

    Conclusion

    For fully supported RTL design in your app, briefly you can follow these steps

    • Replace your app’s all left/right layout properties with start/end which are their equivalents.
    • If you use ViewPager, just migrate ViewPager2.
    • Set text direction “locale” for your text views
    • Add autoMirrored = true in your icons which need to mirroring in RTL design.

    Thank you for reading. If you think the article is useful please clap 👏 and share so more people benefit.

    Keep In Touch

    Sources

    Support Layout Mirroring Developers Android

item.title: Zoom 快速入门 | Jumpstart by Zoom
item.description:
您是否需要在 Web 应用程序中实现视频但不知道从哪里开始？ 想从 Zoom Video SDK 入手，但时间和预算不够？ 要了解更多信息，请查看我们的介绍视频。

对于本教程，我们将使用一个已定义的用例：开发人员需要将会议功能添加到网站以提供客户帮助。

如何使用 Jumpstart

首先，您将转到 Jumpstart 工具并单击“构建应用程序”。 这将带您进入应用程序自定义和下载屏幕：

配置

第一个表单是配置表单，您将使用它来建立您的应用名称和描述。 在您编辑这些内容时，您可以实时预览下载的应用程序的外观。 对于我们的用例，用户将输入他们的公司名称和客户支持用途的简短描述。

特征

配置列右上角的箭头按钮将带您进入下一步和上一步，但您也可以单击左列中的名称直接导航。

接下来，选择可用功能——您可以自定义新应用的外观和功能。 对于我们的用例，我们将保留默认设置。

品牌推广

接下来是品牌化。 您可以在此处更改图像、按钮和上部菜单颜色，并添加指向上部菜单的链接。 我们将更改颜色以匹配相关公司和他们的设计团队为我们的用例提供的图像。

平台

下一个项目是平台。 您只能为测试版选择“Web”，但随着我们开发此工具，其他选项将变得可用。

下载代码

单击后，开始下载可能需要几秒钟。

运行代码

下载代码存档后，解压缩并导航到终端中创建的文件夹。 打开 README.md 以获取特定的启动说明。 在此步骤中，您将需要您的视频 SDK 凭据。

部署你的代码

我们在 Jumpstart 文档中编写了在 Vercel 和 Netlify 上部署 Jumpstart 应用程序的教程。

如果您正在寻找制作 Zoom Video SDK 应用程序，Jumpstart 可能是最好的起点！ 如果您有兴趣在实践环境中学习更多内容，我们将在 6 月 7 日太平洋时间上午 10 点/东部时间下午 1 点举办网络研讨会，您可以在此处注册。

谢谢阅读！



原文:

Do you need to implement video into a web application but don’t know where to start? Want to start with Zoom Video SDK but you’re short on time and budget? Jumpstart is here for you!

Jumpstart is a new tool from the Zoom Developer Tools team that allows you to customize and download a Zoom Video SDK application that will give you a starting point and a foothold into creating your own Zoom Video SDK applications. To learn more check out our introduction video.

For this tutorial, we will be using a defined use case: a developer needs to add meeting functionality to a website for customer assistance purposes.

How to Use Jumpstart

First, you’ll go to the Jumpstart tool and click ‘Build App’. This will take you to the app customization and download screen:

Configuration

The first form is the configuration form, which you’ll use to establish your app name and description. As you edit these, you can see a live preview of what the downloaded app will look like. For our use case, the user would enter their company name and a short description of the customer support uses.

Features

The arrow buttons on the top right of the configuration column will take you to the next and previous steps, but you can also click the names in the left column to navigate directly.

Next, select available features – you can customize the look of your new app and the functionality. For our use case, we’ll leave the settings on default.

Branding

Next up is branding. Here is where you can change the images, the button and upper menu color, and add links to the upper menu. We’ll change the color to match the company in question and images from their design team for our use case.

Platforms

The next item is platforms. You can only select ‘Web’ for the beta, but as we grow this tool, the other selections will become available.

Downloading the Code

The final step for the web interface is to download the Jumpstart code base. Once you click, it can take several seconds for the download to initiate.

Running the Code

Once you have downloaded the code archive, unzip it and navigate to the created folder in your terminal. Open up the README.md for specific launch instructions. You’ll need your Video SDK credentials in this step.

Deploying Your Code

We have written tutorials for deploying your jumpstart app on Vercel and Netlify in the Jumpstart Documentation.

If you’re looking to make Zoom Video SDK applications, Jumpstart might be the best place to start! If you’re interested in learning even more in a hands-on setting, we have a webinar on June 7 at 10 am PT / 1 pm ET that you can register for here.

Thanks for reading!
item.author: nodebotanist

item.title: 思考云； 想想谷歌云平台 | Think Cloud; Think Google Cloud Platform
item.description:
谷歌云平台是谷歌的一套公共云计算服务，用于在谷歌云上运行的计算、存储和应用程序开发。 云管理员、IT 专业人员和软件开发人员可以通过互联网或专用网络连接使用 Google Cloud Platform 服务。

以下是选择 Google Cloud Platform 的五个理由：

谷歌云平台为云数据仓库、高级机器学习和可视化分析以及智能数据处理和数据准备提供了大量的大数据技术创新和服务和框架。 这些无服务器数据分析解决方案能够生成能够改变业务未来的准确见解。

据谷歌称，他们甚至在太平洋下铺设了光缆！ 因此，Google 的网络除了具有极强的可扩展性外，还可以快速、一致地交付。

经过 15 年的完善，用于 Google Apps 的 Google 安全模型非常出色。 相同的安全模型还用于保护存储在 Google Cloud Platform 上的应用程序和数据。

使用 GCP 的 Compute Engine 服务，按每秒使用量计算计费，因此您不必为不用的东西付费

正如谷歌自己所说，用于谷歌云平台的数据中心使用的能源只有典型数据中心消耗的一半。 只要有可能，Google 还会确保使用可再生能源。

作为 Google Cloud 合作伙伴，CloudNow 具备专业知识，可帮助您迁移到 Google Cloud Platform。 今天就联系我们！



原文:

Google Cloud Platform is Google’s suite of public cloud computing services for compute, storage and application development that run on Google Cloud. Google Cloud Platform services can be used by cloud administrators, IT professionals, and software developers over the internet or through a dedicated network connection.

Here are five reasons to choose the Google Cloud Platform:

Google Cloud Platform comes with a slew of big data technology innovations and services and frameworks for cloud data warehousing, advanced machine learning, and visual analytics, along with intelligent data processing and data preparation. These serverless data analytics solutions have the power to generate accurate insights that are capable of transforming the future of business.

According to Google, they have fiber optic cables laid even under the Pacific Ocean! Google’s network, therefore, delivers quickly and consistently, in addition to being extremely scalable.

Perfected over a span of 15 years, Google’s security model — that is used for Google Apps — is exceptional. The same security model is also used to protect applications and data stored on the Google Cloud Platform.

With the Compute Engine services of GCP, billing is calculated per second of usage, so you don’t have to pay for what you don’t use

As stated by Google themselves, data centers used for the Google Cloud Platform use only half as much energy as consumed by a typical data center. Wherever possible, Google also ensures the use of renewable energy.

As a Google Cloud Partner, CloudNow is equipped with the expertise to help you make your move to the Google Cloud Platform. Cloud now offers both cloud migration services and cloud migration consulting services. Get in touch with us today!
item.author: arjun_76

item.title: 在 ReactJS 中使用 Pinata API 将文件发送到 IPFS | Send Files to IPFS using Pinata API in ReactJS
item.description:
各位读者好，我希望你们做得很好。 在本文中，我将讨论如何使用 Pinata API 将文件上传到 IPFS。

在继续之前，让我们先谈谈 IPFS。 许多公司都提供这项服务，例如：Pinata，我们将在本文中进行研究。

首先，要使用 Pinata IPFS，请在 Pinata 上创建一个帐户。 我们需要 API 密钥来使用他们的 API。 因此，在右上角，单击配置文件选项，然后单击 API Keys 选项。

现在，保持 Admin 选项打开，因为您想将文件固定到 IPFS 并输入您想要的任何名称。 它现在将生成一个新的 API_KEY 和一个 SECRET_KEY。 将这两个详细信息存储在 React App 文件夹根目录下的“.env.local”文件中：

 REACT_APP_PINATA_API_KEY =< your api key > REACT_APP_PINATA_API_SECRET =< your secret key >

确保使用以下命令安装 dotenv npm 包：npm i dotenv

所以，让我们定义一个 useState 挂钩来管理文件的内容。

 const [ fileImg , setFileImg ] = useState ( null );

注意：不要忘记 import {useState} from 'react';

接下来我们需要一个函数来检查我们是否有一个空文件，如果没有，我们将使用 Pinata API 发送该文件。

 const sendFileToIPFS = async ( e ) => { if ( fileImg ) { try { const formData = new FormData (); formData . append ( " file " , fileImg ); const resFile = await axios ({ method : " post " , url : " https://api.pinata.cloud/pinning/pinFileToIPFS " , data : formData , headers : { ' pinata_api_key ' : ` ${ process . env . REACT_APP_PINATA_API_KEY } ` , ' pinata_secret_api_key ' : ` ${ process . env . REACT_APP_PINATA_API_SECRET } ` , " Content-Type " : " multipart/form-data " }, }); const ImgHash = `ipfs:// ${ resFile . data . IpfsHash } ` ; sendJSONtoIPFS ( ImgHash ) } catch ( error ) { console . log ( " Error sending File to IPFS: " ) console . log ( error ) } } }

最后，让我们定义我们要返回的内容。 我们在提交表单时调用函数 sendFiletoIPFS()，文件的值将是 e.target.files[0]。

 return ( < form onSubmit = { sendFileToIPFS } > < input type = " file " onChange = {( e ) => setFileImg ( e . target . files [ 0 ])} required /> < button type = ' submit ' > Mint NFT < /button> < /form > )

您可以在 Pinata 的 API 文档中了解有关如何发送 JSON 数据、从 IPFS 中删除文件等的更多信息。
我希望你学会了如何在 React 应用程序中将文件发送到 Pinata IPFS。 如果您有任何疑问，请随时与我联系：

如果你想用 IPFS API 铸造一个 NFT，你可以在这里查看我的项目页面：
在那之前，继续黑客攻击！😊



原文:

Hi fellow readers, I hope you’re doing good. Web3 is an amazing space and every day there is something new to learn no matter how experienced you are. In this article, I’m gonna talk about how we can upload files to IPFS using Pinata API.

Before moving on, let's talk a little about IPFS. IPFS/ InterPlanetary File System is a protocol and peer-to-peer network for storing and sharing data in a distributed file system. Many companies provide this service, for example: Pinata, which we’re gonna work on in this article.

First, to work with Pinata IPFS, create an account on Pinata. We need API keys to work with their API. Therefore, in the top right corner, click on the profile option and click on the API Keys option.

Now, keep the Admin option on because you’d like to pin files to the IPFS and enter any name you’d like. It will now generate a new API_KEY along with a SECRET_KEY. Store both the details in a ".env.local" file present at the root of the React App folder as:

REACT_APP_PINATA_API_KEY=<your api key>
REACT_APP_PINATA_API_SECRET=<your secret key>

Make sure to install dotenv npm package using: npm i dotenv

So, let's define a useState hook to manage the content of the file.

 const [fileImg, setFileImg] = useState(null);

Note: Don’t forget import {useState} from ‘react’;

The next thing we need is a function to check whether we are having an empty file, if not we will be sending that file using the Pinata API.

 const sendFileToIPFS = async (e) => { if (fileImg) { try { const formData = new FormData(); formData.append("file", fileImg); const resFile = await axios({ method: "post", url: "https://api.pinata.cloud/pinning/pinFileToIPFS", data: formData, headers: { 'pinata_api_key': `${process.env.REACT_APP_PINATA_API_KEY}`, 'pinata_secret_api_key': `${process.env.REACT_APP_PINATA_API_SECRET}`, "Content-Type": "multipart/form-data" }, }); const ImgHash = `ipfs://${resFile.data.IpfsHash}`; sendJSONtoIPFS(ImgHash) } catch (error) { console.log("Error sending File to IPFS: ") console.log(error) } } }

Finally, let's define what we’re gonna return. We are calling a function sendFiletoIPFS() when the form is submitted, and the value of the file is gonna be e.target.files[0].

return(
<form onSubmit={sendFileToIPFS}>
<input type="file" onChange={(e) =>setFileImg(e.target.files[0])} required />
<button type='submit' >Mint NFT</button>  </form>
)

You can learn more about how to send JSON data, remove files from IPFS, etc in the API documentation by Pinata.
I hope you learned how to send files to Pinata IPFS in a React Application. If you have any queries, feel free to connect with me on:

If you want to mint an NFT with IPFS API, you can take a look at my project page here:
https://github.com/FidalMathew/Voting_system_blockchain/blob/main/client-votingSystem/src/SendNft.jsx

Till then, keep hacking!😊

item.author: sherrydays

item.title: Ruby on Rails 与 Node.js：2022 年最好的是什么？ | Ruby on Rails vs Node.js: What is the best in 2022?
item.description:
发表于为您的网站选择前端技术堆栈并不难——CSS、HTML5 和 JavaScript 是显而易见的选择。 另一方面，后端技术更加通用，Ruby on Rails 与 Node.js 最近成为最流行的后端语言。 为了帮助您在 Ruby on Rails 与 Node.js 之间做出选择，我们将提供我们使用 Ruby on Rails 与 Node.js 的经验，包括团队确定的所有优缺点。 我们还将讨论用例和实际应用。 在本文结束时，您将知道 Node.js 或 Ruby on Rails 是否最适合您的项目。

但是，它是一个运行时环境，允许用户将客户端代码转换为服务器端代码。 它是一个基于 Chrome V8 引擎的框架，是现存最快的引擎之一，这也是 runtime 转换速度快的原因。

用 Ruby 语言只需 5-10 行就可以完成同样的事情，需要大量的 Java 行。

让我们通过查看它们的优缺点和使用案例来比较Ruby on Rails 和 Node.js。

在哪里使用 Node.js 更好？

它允许您与后端和浏览器共享代码并同时处理多个任务。 让我们来看看这些优势如何在 Web 应用程序开发的各种元素中发挥作用。

服务器端开发

它采用长轮询技术，允许应用程序处理多个请求。 在前端和后端都使用 JavaScript 的可能性可以使开发更加高效和直接。 如果您以前使用过JS ，并且对学习 Node.js 以运行后端的过程感兴趣，那将不是一个挑战。

如果用户正在搜索某些内容，网站应用程序会使用 REST API 来提供来自源的结果。 微服务

它的非阻塞请求处理算法和事件驱动方法允许我们连接多个服务并同时处理许多内部请求。 微服务必须不断地进行通信，这就是确保数据处理速度至关重要的原因。

微服务架构和 Node.js 专注于执行速度和有效性。 微服务架构的全部意义在于允许开发人员通过将功能分解为可管理的组件来创建可扩展且高效的平台——Node.js 通过利用快速 V8 引擎和非阻塞处理来帮助实现这一想法。

使用 Node.js 的公司

由于 Node.js 的性能和响应能力，企业正在转向使用它。 对于一些公司来说，向 Node.js 的过渡伴随着向微服务架构的切换。

例如，Netflix 去中心化了其网站平台，并使用 Node.js 作为其主要的后端应用程序来实现这一点。 这导致应用程序的响应能力和可扩展性更高。 同样的事情是通过 PayPal 开发组实现的。 该公司使用 Node 进行原型设计和开发 - 该团队报告说，团队效率和应用程序性能的速度有了惊人的提高。

必须弥补 JS 无法在服务器端工作的缺陷，因此提供了有限的体验——这就是原因。

如果我们谈论的是快速流程，Node.js 会快速执行它们。 但是，如果您正在讨论耗时的过程，Node 不会将事件视为事件。 一项长期任务可以阻止所有其他任务。

这种方法会导致高 CPU 消耗和速度急剧下降。 因此，当您不使用消耗大量 CPU 的任务加载应用程序时，大多数 Node.js 的优势都会有所帮助，一旦您越过这条线，Node.js 就会失去许多优势。

安全问题不容易解决。

这个开源管理平台为开发人员提供了可重用的代码、插件和附加组件，增加了应用程序的多功能性。

但是许多团队并没有意识到与 npm 包相关的风险。 问题是 Node.js 团队最近才开始检查模块是否存在安全问题。 大多数 NPM 包，例如 hard-cookie、js.com 和许多其他包，都存在依赖关系、漏洞和潜在安全风险的空间。

在哪里更喜欢使用 Ruby on Rails？

您可以使用比 Java 和 Node.js 所需的更少的代码行来编写复杂的功能。

全栈网络应用

该框架建立在其模型、视图和控制器框架之上。 定义控制器、视图和路由对于允许应用程序响应请求是必要的。 建立 MVC 框架可能是一个耗时的过程； 但是，最后，代码组织良好且易于阅读。 最后，您将拥有一个高效、易于编辑和维护的软件。

内容管理系统

然而，Ruby on Rails 是在最近几个月才进入该领域，并具有许多竞争优势。 可以将整个数据库和表映射为不同的对象，并且您可以使用几行代码来引用一大块功能。

原型制作

假设您正在开发产品的最小可行版本。 主要目标是运营效率和组织良好的开发流程。 该代码可以提供开源库一半以上的功能。

许多开发人员想知道 Ruby 和 Ruby on Rails 的编写过程的有效性，以及为什么语言和框架不为人所知？ 虽然可以肯定该语言会赢得低效率，但它的受欢迎程度并不完全是一流的。

性能速度

当我们比较 Ruby on Rails 与 Node.js 和 Node.js 的速度时，即使是最专业的 Ruby on Rails 开发人员也承认该框架很慢。 尽管它在每个版本中都提高了速度，但它仍然比 Node.js 小。 性能缓慢的原因有很多。

因此，硬件更难以有效地处理和读取。 删除另一个转换步骤可以提高应用程序的性能速度。 相比之下，Ruby 仅在最新版本中实现了 JIT。 该技术并不完美，即使当前版本的 JIT 降低了代码处理的速度。

但是，当您比较 Ruby on Rails 与 Node.js 的性能时，Rails 不会是您的首选。

垃圾回收无效

事实上，被垃圾收集的语言往往会变慢。 在任何时候，开发人员都可能注意到他们的应用程序中内存使用的突然增加和性能的显着下降。 应用程序容量越高，每个版本的垃圾收集器消耗的内存就越多。

对于 Ruby，GC.disable 将停止垃圾收集。 还有一些用于处理与垃圾收集器相关的内存泄漏的库，但它们主要用作临时解决方案。

无静态打字系统

如果我们将 Ruby 与 Node.js 进行比较，那么缺少静态类型系统是主要缺点之一。 使用 Node.js，TypeScript 允许添加 JS 不使用的功能。 这些功能以典型的 JavaScript 变量的形式编写和呈现。 语言处理其本地和非本地部分的方式没有区别。

它还允许您使用特定功能来增加应用程序与特定浏览器的兼容性。 它允许您键入代码、排列特性和创建新函数，而在 Ruby 中，这些函数不可用。

阅读更多： Django vs Ruby on rails – 哪个更好？

节点.js
由于 Node.js 的固有特性，该技术有效地利用了单个处理器内核和内存。

许多企业在其移动应用后端堆栈中使用 Node.js。 例如，与 Java 应用程序相比，技术巨头 Paypal 的每秒请求数增加了，Paypal 的平均响应时间也增加了 35%。

对象和其他基本变量的高内存消耗

由于 Rails 的字符串操作和 JSON 翻译，LinkedIn 的性能显着下降。 改用 Node.js 后，该公司将服务器容量从 30 台减少到 3 台，并且在某些情况下性能提高了 20 倍。

但是，这并不意味着 Ruby on Rails 不值得成为后端技术。 例如，备受期待的 Ruby 3 包括许多性能增强，例如并发和 MJIT。 此外，并不是每个数字应用程序都需要 Node.js 的超快速度，主要因素是服务的用户数量。

可扩展性

节点.js
可扩展性是指每分钟可以有效处理各种用户请求的应用程序。 它的最新版本具有集群模块功能并支持微服务。 这意味着将应用程序分解成更小的部分。 微服务架构可以根据需求扩大或缩小。 此外，Node.js 允许您在多个 CPU 内核上进行负载平衡，从而使应用程序可在单个服务器上进行扩展。

据报道，沃尔玛已成功将其旧系统改为使用 Node.js 作为后端技术的微服务。 该公司的移动转化率增长了 98%。

红宝石
然而，Airbnb、GitHub 和 Shopify 等几家拥有庞大客户群的顶级科技公司正在其技术堆栈中使用 RoR。

阅读更多： Ruby on Rails 与 Python：有什么区别。

发展速度

节点.js
该技术的异步和事件驱动方面对于初学者来说可能具有挑战性。 但是，根据 2021 年 Stack Overflow 调查，Node.js 使用 JavaScript，这是全球最常用和流行的编程语言。

使用 Node.js，开发人员可以使用相同的编程语言（例如，前端的 React Native 和后端的 Node.js）开发移动应用程序的前端和后端。 众多框架、第三方库（感谢 npm 存储库的庞大存储库）和学习资源使开发人员能够快速开发移动应用程序。

例如，TypeScript 是微软开发的一种编程语言，用于帮助使用 Node.js、React 或 React Native 开发大型应用程序。

红宝石
它带有对象关系映射 (ORM) 系统，允许您连接业务逻辑和数据层，以快速创建和测试完整且功能正常的原型。

此外，您可以通过一些命令使用完整的脚本、模块和包管理系统来构建复杂的应用程序。 此外，还有各种允许快速开发的第三方库或 Ruby gem。

社区支持

节点.js
有多种社区论坛可用于 Node.js 移动应用程序开发，包括 Github、Stack Overflow 和 Reddit。 在 StackOverflow 对 2021 年开发人员进行的调查中，Node.js 跻身顶级技术之列。 此外，Facebook 和 Google 等几家顶级科技公司都参与了 Node.js 的开发过程，这使其成为值得信赖且可靠的开源技术。

有几个社区论坛可用于 Rails 移动应用程序开发，例如 Github、Slack 和 Gitter。 他们经常更新源代码、修复错误并解决安全漏洞。 使用 Rails，确定要合并到应用程序中的确切代码（或 gem）相对容易。

测试支持

节点.js
一些自动化测试框架，如 Mocha、Jasmine、Jest、Chai 和基于移动的测试工具，如 webdriverIO 可用。

事实上，Ruby 社区正在积极参与测试过程和自动化。 测试是 Ruby on Rails 不可或缺的一部分，使开发人员在构建后端时更容易检查他们编写的代码。 因为 Ruby 是基于 MVC 架构构建的，所以它可以在开发 coon 的同时自动生成框架测试代码。 阅读更多： Ruby on Rails 与 Javascript：哪个更好？

什么是移动应用程序的最佳后端？

移动应用程序的后端在很大程度上取决于移动应用程序的目的和业务的短期和长期目标。 选择最佳选项的秘诀在于开发人员使用特定技术或语言编写后端的能力。 即使在顶级解决方案中，没有经验的开发人员也可能创建低质量的移动应用程序后端。

带有 Node.js 后端的移动应用程序
使用 Node.js，可以创建高度可扩展的应用程序，这些应用程序可以处理用户的多个并发请求。

以下是您可以在后端使用 Node.js 的应用程序类型：

社交媒体应用程序：这些应用程序必须管理与用户的许多交互。 因此，效率和可扩展性是创建应用程序时的主要方面。 目前，Twitter 和 LinkedIn 等主要社交媒体网站都在使用 Node.js。 发布应用程序：Cartovera 可以成为在分布式服务器上实现 Node.js 的绝佳示例。 单页应用程序：此类程序仅在 Web 浏览器的一页上运行。 实时聊天应用程序：远程协助是使用 Node.js、React Native、MongoDB 和各种其他技术构建的实时聊天应用程序。 在线学习平台：Quizlet 每月服务的学习者超过 1800 万，活跃学习者超过 3000 万。 后端使用 Ruby on Rails 的移动应用程序

借助大量的 Ruby gem，开发人员可以立即构建各种功能。

以下是一些示例，您可以将 RoR 作为移动应用程序的后端：

电子商务应用程序：有多种 Ruby gem 可以涵盖从安全性到身份验证的大部分功能。 需要快速开发以满足最后期限的应用程序。 广泛使用 API 的软件即服务 (SaaS) 项目

项目依赖于广泛的数据库，例如 Github 及其所有存储库。

结论

然而，它们都不是100%完美的。 它的非阻塞过程和事件驱动的语法使 Node.js 具有非常灵敏和灵活的后端语言。 但是，Ruby 无法应对这样的负载，并且您将面临随机服务器崩溃的风险。

代码质量比 Node.js 好得多，开发人员不需要重复他们的代码或使用过多的规范。 该语言在性能上没有那么高效，但开发过程是最高效的，不仅仅是与 Node.js 相比。

哪个更有效：Ruby on Rails 与 Node.js？ 两种语言都可以用于不同类型的项目。 对于将开发速度视为首要任务的团队（Saas 开发、MVP 等），Ruby 是一个绝佳的选择。 如果您正在考虑为后端开发人员构建应用程序的应用程序，请告诉我们您的想法。 我们的 Bestarion 开发团队将确定项目的优先级，并帮助您选择最适合您需求的最佳工具。

Kent Ezra A. Betita - Kent Ezra A. Betita - May 30 5月30日



原文:

AllaboutTech

Posted on Jun 1

Choosing a front-end technology stack for your website isn’t tough – CSS, HTML5, and JavaScript are obvious choices. On the other side, back-end technology is far more versatile, Ruby on Rails vs Node.js have recently emerged as the most popular back-end languages. To assist you in deciding between Ruby on Rails vs Node.js, we’ll offer our experience working with Ruby on Rails vs Node.js, including all pros and cons identified by the team. We’ll also discuss use cases and practical applications. By the end of this article, you’ll know if Node.js or Ruby on Rails is the best fit for your project.

Overview of Node.js and Ruby on Rails

Node.js isn’t a language; however, it is a runtime environment that allows users to transform client-side code into server-side. Node converts JavaScript to machine code, which is quickly processed by the hardware. It is a framework based on the Chrome V8 engine, and it’s one of the fastest engines that exist, which is why runtime’s fast conversion speed.

Ruby on Rails is a framework for developing websites. Rails were designed to assist handle internal logic, routines, and data computing. Ruby on Rails is known for its efficiency in code and its speed. The same thing that takes a plethora of lines of Java can be accomplished with just 5-10 lines in Ruby.

Let’s compare Ruby on Rails vs Node.js by looking at their pros, cons, and usage cases.

Where is It Better to Use Node.js?

Node.js is a command-line environment that allows you to run the identical JS code that you’re creating for your front end. It lets you share codes with the back-end and the browser and handle multiple tasks simultaneously. Let’s examine how these advantages are helpful in various elements of web application development.

Server-side development

Node is adept at establishing efficient communications between the client and the server. It employs a long-polling technique that allows the application to process multiple requests. Node.js does not wait for every query to be processed before starting handling new incoming ones.

Node.js is ideal for real-time applications such as messengers, online games, chatrooms, and real-time collaboration platforms. The possibility of using JavaScript both on the front-end and the back-end can make development more efficient and more straightforward. If you have worked previously using JS and are interested in the process of learning Node.js to run back-ends will not be a challenge.

API development

Node.js is a preferred software for developing REST API development. Representational State Transfer (REST) – it’s an architecture that allows users to get output to their web requests rapidly. The basic principles of REST development are to be uniform, stateless, client-server, have a layered system, offer a cacheable architecture, and respond on-demand.

REST APIs are beneficial for web development. If a user is searching for something, a website application uses REST APIs to provide results from the source. REST APIs link the user’s input to the program’s code, creating a communication channel. Node.js, thanks to its fast processing speed for requests and JS technology stack, is the obvious first option for developing REST APIs.

Microservices

Node.js’s architecture is in perfect alignment with the microservices’ structure. Its non-blocking request processing algorithms and event-driven approach allow us to connect multiple services and handle many internal requests simultaneously. Microservices have to be in communication constantly, and that’s why ensuring the speed of data processing is crucial.

Microservice architecture and Node.js are focused on the speed of execution and effectiveness. The whole point of a microservices architecture is to allow developers to create scalable and efficient platforms by breaking down functionality into manageable components—Node.js assists in bringing this idea to life by utilizing a fast V8 engine and non-blocking processing.

Companies that use Node.js

Businesses are switching to Node.js due to its performance and responsiveness. Node.js is currently being used in LinkedIn, Uber, Medium, New York Times, and Hapi. For some companies, a transition to Node.js is coupled with switching to the microservice architecture.

For example, Netflix decentralized its website platform and employed Node.js as its primary back-end application to accomplish this. This resulted in the app’s more responsiveness and scalability. The same thing was implemented through the PayPal development group. The company utilized Node for prototyping and development -The team reported an astounding improvement in the efficiency of the team and the speed of the app’s performance.

The Issue with Node.js

Node.js is not an ideal back-end tool. JavaScript is a tool for front-end development that’s not well-adapted to specific development aspects on servers, and it’s designed not suitable for back-end functionality. Has to compensate for JS’s inability to work on the server-side and, as a result, offers a somewhat limited experience — here’s why.

Processor consuming tasks for Node.js applications

Node.js is a single-threaded language, meaning that it will only process just one task at a given time unless you create them in a sequence of events. If we’re talking about quick processes, Node.js executes them quickly. But, if you are discussing time-consuming processes, Node does not treat the event as an event. One long-term task can block all the others.

This approach results in high CPU consumption and a drastic reduction in speed. Thus, most Node.js benefits are helpful when you don’t load the app with tasks that consume a lot of CPU, and once you cross that line, Node.js loses many of its advantages.

Security concerns aren’t easy to resolve.

Node.js is well-known for its extensive ecosystem. This open-source management platform provides developers with reusable code, plugins, and add-ons, increasing the versatility of the application.

Node.js contains a wealth of NPM packages – Using ready components reduces time and could improve code quality. But many teams aren’t aware of the risks associated with the npm package. The issue is that the Node.js team just recently started checking modules for security concerns. The majority of the NPM packages, such as hard-cookie, js.com, and many others, have dependencies, vulnerabilities, and room for potential security risks.

Where is It Preferable to Use Ruby on Rails?

Ruby and Rails are utilized as a back-end framework for web applications, and it’s renowned for their effectiveness and scalability. You can write sophisticated functionality using fewer code lines than what you’d have to need in Java and Node.js.

Full-stack web application

Ruby on Rails is a highly structured framework for web development. Rails have a lot of rules on how code should be organized. The framework is built on its Model, View, and Controller framework. Defining a controller, view, and route is necessary to allow an application to respond to requests. Establishing an MVC framework can be a time-consuming process; however, in the end, the code is well-organized and easy to read. In the end, You’ll have an efficient, easy to edit and maintain.

Content management systems

Ruby on Rails is not yet the top option for content management systems development, and PHP remains the preferred option. However, Ruby on Rails entered the field in the last few months and has many competitive advantages. Ruby on Rails allows rapid writing of rich functionality because of its high abstraction levels. It is possible to map whole databases and tables as distinct objects, and You can refer to a big chunk of functionality with several lines of code.

Prototyping

Ruby on Rails allows you to write code faster and implement built-in features available in the ecosystem. Suppose you’re developing the minimally viable version of a product. The main goal is operational efficiency and well-organized development processes. Ruby on Rails offers multiple command-line code builders that let developers write code from scratch. The code can provide more than half the features of an open-source library.

The issue with Ruby on Rails.

Many developers are wondering about the efficacy of the writing process for Ruby as well as Ruby on Rails can be and how is it that the language and the framework aren’t more well-known? While it is certain that the language will win inefficiency, its popularity isn’t exactly stellar.

Speed of performance

When we look at the speeds of Ruby on Rails vs. Node.js and Node.js, even the most experts Ruby on Rails developers acknowledge that the framework is slow. Although it improves its speed with each release, it’s still smaller than Node.js. There are many reasons for the slow performance.

Ruby is an interpreter tool that means that the code isn’t converted into a machine-readable version. It’s, therefore, more difficult for hardware to process and read efficiently. Node.js provides its users with the Just In Time compilation which permits turning JS source code to a By code, not the machine language. Removing another conversion step boosts the speed of performance of the application. Ruby is, by contrast, implemented JIT only in the most recent versions. The technology is not perfect, even if the current version of JIT reduces the speed of code processing.

Ruby allows you to run multiple threads at once, which means that the issue of handling tasks that are CPU intensive isn’t as severe as it is using Node. But when you’re the comparison of Ruby on Rails vs. Node.js performance, Rails won’t be your first option.

Garbage collection ineffectiveness

It’s a fact that languages that are garbage-collected tend to slow down. At any point, developers may notice an abrupt increase in the use of memory in their applications and a significant decrease in performance. The higher the application capacity and the more significant memory that every version of the garbage collector will consume.

For Ruby, GC.disable will stop garbage collection. There are also libraries for handling garbage-collector-related memory leaks, but they mainly act as a temporary solution.

No Static Typing System

If we compare Ruby with Node.js, the absence of a static type system is among the main disadvantages. With Node.js, TypeScript allows added features that JS doesn’t use. These features are written and presented in the form of typical JavaScript variables. There is no distinction in how the language handles its native and non-native parts.

TypeScript is a must-have to improve code reliability, aiding team communication and documentation of the development process. It also lets you use specific features to increase the application’s compatibility with specific browsers. It allows you to type code, arrange features, and create new functions, and in Ruby, these functions aren’t available.

Read more: Django vs Ruby on rails – Which is the better?

Ruby on Rails vs Node.js

Is Node.js faster than Ruby?

Node.js
One of the most impressive advantages of Node.js is its speed. The Single-threaded, asynchronous, non-blocking execution of the Javascript V8 engine dramatically contributes to the fast performance of Node’s system. Node.js can handle a vast number of concurrent requests. Due to the inherent characteristics of Node.js, the technology utilizes a single processor core and memory effectively.

Numerous businesses are using Node.js in their mobile app back-end stacks. For instance, technology giant Paypal has increased its requests per second compared with its Java application, and Paypal also reported an increase of 35% in the average response time.

Ruby on Rails
Ruby on Rails, specifically Ruby (the language built by Rails ), is generally regarded as slower than Node.js. There is a myriad of reasons for Ruby’s slow .performance.

Ruby’s Garbage Collector (GC) uses a slow algorithm that stops the application when garbage collection is in progress.

High memory consumption for objects and other basic variables

LinkedIn has experienced significant performance declines due to Rails’ string manipulation and JSON translation. After the switch to Node.js, The company reduced server capacity from 30 down to three and gained 20x faster performance in some cases.

However, it doesn’t mean that Ruby on Rails isn’t worthy of being a back-end tech. Ruby’s developers are actively involved in resolving any issues with performance. For instance, the highly-anticipated Ruby 3 included numerous performance enhancements like concurrently and MJIT. Additionally, not every digital application demands the super-fast speeds of Node.js, and the main factor is the number of users that will be served.

Scalability

Node.js
Scalability refers to an application that can efficiently handle various user requests per minute. There are numerous options in Node.js that allow scaling. Its most recent version has cluster module capability and supports microservices. This means breaking the application down into smaller parts. The microservices architecture can scale up or down according to the demands. Additionally, Node.js lets you load balance on multiple CPU cores, making the app scalable on single servers.

Based on reports, Walmart has successfully changed its old system to microservices using Node.js as the back-end technology. The company has experienced an increase of 98% in mobile-based conversion.

Ruby
RoR supports scalability by improving applications’ performance and infrastructure, so you’ll need to add some resources to reap the scalability benefits. Yet, several top tech companies with huge customer bases, such as Airbnb, GitHub, and Shopify, are using RoR within their technology stacks.

Read more: Ruby on Rails vs Python: What’s the Difference.

Speed of development

Node.js
The asynchronous and event-driven aspects of the technology might be challenging for beginners to grasp. But, Node.js uses JavaScript, the most used and popular programming language globally, according to the Stack Overflow Survey 2021.

With Node.js, developers can develop the front-end and back-end of mobile applications by using this same programming language (e.g., React Native on the front and Node.js at the rear). The numerous frameworks, libraries from third parties (thanks to the massive repository of the npm repository), and learning resources allow developers to develop mobile apps fast.

For instance, There’s TypeScript -the programming language developed by Microsoft to aid in developing large-scale applications using Node.js, React or React Native.

Ruby
RoR is specifically designed to speed up prototyping and developing applications. It comes with the Object Relational Mapping (ORM) system that allows you to connect business logic and the data layer to create and test a complete and functioning prototype quickly.

Ruby language is simple and easy to write. Additionally, you can build an intricate application in just some commands with complete scripts, modules, and a package management system. Additionally, there are a variety of third-party libraries or Ruby gems that allow rapid development.

Community Support

Node.js
A variety of community forums are available for Node.js mobile application development, including Github, Stack Overflow, and Reddit. In the survey of 2021 developers conducted by StackOverflow, Node.js scored among the top technologies. In addition, several of the top tech companies, such as Facebook and Google, have been involved in the Node.js development process, which makes it a trusted and reliable open-source technology.

Ruby on Rails
RoR also has a vibrant community of innovative developers. Several community forums are available for Rails mobile app development, such as Github, Slack, and Gitter. Rails developers are actively engaged in improving the existing functionalities and new feature development. They frequently update the source code, fix bugs, and address security vulnerabilities. With Rails, it’s relatively easy to identify the exact piece of code (or gem) you want to incorporate into your application.

Testing Support

Node.js
Node.js provides full test automation and debugging tools with its vast library of libraries from third parties. Several automated testing frameworks, such as Mocha, Jasmine, Jest, Chai, and mobile-based testing tools, such as webdriverIO are available.

Ruby on Rails
It is a fact that the Ruby community is actively engaged in the testing process and automation. Testing is an integral part of Ruby on Rails, making it easier for developers to check the code they write as they build the back-end. Because Ruby is built upon the MVC architecture, it can automatically generate skeleton test code while developing the coon. There are a variety of testing frameworks for RoR, e.g., Rspec, Minitest, or Cucumber.

Read more: Ruby on Rails vs Javascript: Which is Better?

What Is the Best Back-end for a Mobile Application?

The back-end of mobile applications is heavily dependent on the purpose of the mobile application and the business’s short-term and long-term goals. Node.js and RoR are among the top back-end technologies used for Android and iOS app development.

The secret to selecting the best option is the developers’ ability to write the back-end using a particular technology or language. Unexperienced developers could create low-quality mobile app back-ends even in top solutions.

Mobile apps with Node.js back-en
With Node.js, it is possible to create highly scalable applications that can handle the processing of multiple concurrent requests by users.

Here are the types of applications in which you can make use of Node.js for the back-end:

Social media Applications: These apps must manage many interactions with users. So, efficiency and scalability are the main aspects when creating apps. Currently, Node.js is used by major social media sites such as Twitter and LinkedIn. Publishing applications: Cartovera can be an excellent example of Node.js implemented on distributed servers. Single-page applications: Such programs are only run on one page on the web browser. Gmail illustrates this, which displays the latest messages on one page. Real-time chat applications: Remote assistance is live chat applications built in Node.js, React Native, MongoDB, and various other technologies. E-learning platform: Quizlet has served more than 18 million learners per month and has over 30 million active learners. Mobile apps with Ruby on Rails on the back-end

Ruby on Rails is great for speeding up prototyping. With an abundance of Ruby gems, developers can build various features right off the bat.

Here are some examples in which you can rely on RoR as the back-end for your mobile application:

E-commerce applications: There are a variety of Ruby gems that can cover the majority of the functionality, from security to authentication. Shopify utilizes Ruby on Rails. Applications that require rapid development to meet deadlines. Software as a Service (SaaS) projects that make use of APIs extensively

Projects rely on extensive databases, such as Github and all its repositories.

Conclusions

Node.js, along with Ruby, are both extremely powerful for back-end development. However, neither of them is 100% perfect. Node.js’ primary selling point is its ability to use JavaScript and high-performance speed for the entire technology stack. Node.js compares to Ruby on Rails performance differs in speed and processing capabilities.

Node.js effectively handles operations that are short-term and swiftly responds to user input. Its non-blocking process and an event-driven syntax make Node.js extraordinarily responsive and flexible back-end languages. Node.js is a great web-socket client and efficiently handles up to 10,000 simultaneous requests. However, Ruby cannot cope with such loads, and you’ll risk a random server crash.

Ruby on Rails and Ruby are recognized for their excellent design (syntax is simple and easy to master). The code quality is much better than that of Node.js developers aren’t required to repeat their code or use excessive specifications. The language isn’t as efficient in performance, but the development process is among the most efficient, not just when compared to Node.

Which is more effective: Ruby on Rails vs Node.js? Both languages can be used for different kinds of projects. Ruby is an excellent choice for teams who see the speed of development as their top prioritization (Saas Development, MVP, etc.). Node.js development tools are the best solution for real-time web and single-page apps.

If you’re thinking of an application for back-end developers to build your application, let us know the idea you have in mind. Our Bestarion development team will determine the project’s priorities and aid you in choosing the best tool that will fit your needs as the glove.

Kent Ezra A. Betita - May 30

item.title: 越南十大外包 Ruby on Rails 软件开发公司 | Top 10 Outsource Ruby on Rails Software Development Companies in Viet Nam
item.description:
后端组件通常是通过 Ruby on Rails 开发人员设计的，它们将应用程序连接到各种（通常是外部的）Web 服务，并帮助前端开发人员将他们的工作连接到应用程序。

如果您搜索得足够仔细，就会发现有很多 Ruby on Rails 开发团队。 但是，很难找到具有高质量和良好信誉的团队。

在下面的文章中，我们将通过介绍越南排名前 10 位的 Ruby On Rails 软件开发公司来帮助您选择最合适的公司，并帮助您就未来的合作做出最佳决策！ 此列表基于 Clutch.co。

什么是 Ruby on Rails？

换句话说，Ruby on Rails 是一个模型视图控制器 (MVC)，它还提供了可用于构建和开发基于 Web 的应用程序的基本数据库结构。 尤其是，Ruby 是一种编程语言，Rails 是一个专门设计用于与 Ruby 完美配合的 Web 应用程序框架。 然而，Ruby 的主要问题是，在 Rails 发布之前，它并未明确设计为在 Internet 上使用。 许多流行的网站都是使用 Ruby on Rails 开发的，包括 SoundCloud、GitHub、Shopify、Airbnb、Bloomberg、Twitter……

阅读更多：外包软件开发成功的 6 大技巧

越南——外包 Ruby on Rails 项目的理想目的地

在全球技术和软件领域正在发展的许多国家中，越南是通过三个主要因素排名靠前的国家之一：

劳动力目前接近人口的“黄金高峰”。

很明显，自战争与和平结束以来，越南人口中近一半以上的人年龄在 30 岁以下，具有积极的态度、高智商和受过良好教育. 此外，教育过程是越南教育系统的一大特色。 它也受到国外专家的推崇，专注于数学研究和技术支持IT行业的技术。

良好的英语沟通能力和演讲能力

良好的英语演讲和交流实际上是一项要求。 选择越南的基本要求是在学习和与最知名的 IT 人员一起工作时使用英语的一致性。 越南从一开始就将英语教育和测评的诸多要求纳入当前“时代潮流”的职业教育学校。

优秀的教育计划

越南教育系统每年培训超过 280,000 名具有专业和软技能的学生。 因此，他们可以准备好专业地进入劳动力市场。 信息和通信技术专业有 30,000-40,000 名毕业生，这是向全球公司外包软件服务的主要来源。

越南 10 大 Ruby on Rails 软件开发公司

阿达摩数码

该公司基于对商业世界的广泛了解、高度发达的技术能力、以质量为导向的交付方法以及对市场最新趋势的深入了解，为其客户提供快速、高效和具有成本效益的解决方案。

该公司是软件全栈开发、技术咨询和基于云的开发的专家。

该公司由战略家、设计师和技术人员组成，他们充满激情、灵感和对质量的长期承诺，利用越南的工程人才将尖端技术推向市场。

韦弗利软件

在他们不断扩大的软件开发团队的帮助下，他们在乌克兰、越南和玻利维亚设有办事处。 他们有可能将您的业务愿景和想法转化为未来将产生巨大影响的产品。

该公司现在在玻利维亚、美国、澳大利亚、乌克兰、越南和玻利维亚拥有超过 250 人的全球团队。 他们的目标是创造能够激发创意的产品，为全球问题提供真正的解决方案并改变人们的生活，这是公司工作中一切的指导原则。

西贡科技

西贡科技已经运营了十多年。 随着世界信息技术产业的发展和建立，特别是在越南，西贡科技一直在越南软件行业排名前 15 位的公司中占据突出地位，拥有超过 100 名受过高等教育和技术熟练的员工。 西贡科技始终竭尽全力为客户提供最有效的产品。

凭借超过 6 年的 IT 咨询和外包经验，BeetSoft 在发展其员工的专业技能和帮助公司在其特定专业领域取得成功方面发挥着重要作用。 协和亚洲

自 2011 年越南成立以来，CO-WELL Asia 已显着扩张。450 多名员工在其位于河内和岘港的两个越南办事处工作，而 100 多名员工在东京和宫崎工作。 该公司目前正在为全球客户开发超过 250 个软件开发项目，包括商业解决方案、电子商务、AR-VR 和软件测试。 这些项目中有 80% 是针对大型企业客户的。 该公司为合作伙伴和客户提供 IT 基础，以最大限度地提高他们的业务，正如其座右铭所述：“在您的成功背后”。

瑞利亚软件

该公司为初创公司提供 MVP，并为企业提供定制的软件解决方案，以确保稳定性、可扩展性和出色的用户体验。 自 2011 年以来，他们已经为 10 多个行业的移动和 Web 平台创建了 300 多个应用程序。这使 Relia Software 在为其客户提供完整解决方案方面具有优势。

他们在 InspiLab 拥有自己的业务和产品。 他们意识到维持出色工作所面临的困难。 贝斯塔里翁

他们已经能够提供外包服务，特别是对 Ruby on Rails、Golang、Linux 上的 C 和 .NET 软件开发的全栈支持。 开发方法的标准化以及自动化工具、框架和框架的帮助是他们的主要能力之一。 从概念到最终确定，VniTeam 协助项目经理和发起人完成数字化流程的每一步

识别业务、营销和销售目标以及限制 收集需求并创建列表 实施和创建品牌营销、广告和营销策略 从基本业务概念创建概念（IA 和线框/模型） 设计响应式用户界面、体验和系统架构 应用程序、网站和软件的开发以敏捷和测试驱动的方式完成。 监控质量并确保其符合国际标准（和用户测试），监控和部署正在进行或适当的维护（+SEO）。 数据分析可以帮助微调和改进结果。
